name: Wiley Nickel
subject: Peter, can you please read my op-ed about Republican gerrymandering in Texas - and how we can fight back?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wiley Nickel for North Carolina
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wiley-em-26fund [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wiley-em-26fund]
Peter, Republican gerrymandering in North Carolina handed Donald Trump control of Congress in the last election. Now Republican gerrymandering in Texas is set to hand Donald Trump control of the next Congress. We can't let that happen.

Democrats must match Republican gerrymandering — for now, at least.

I wrote an op-ed in the Raleigh News & Observer [https://www.newsobserver.com/opinion/article311747810.html] outlining how we got here and how we can fight back. It's copied below, and I hope you'll take a moment to read it. The future of our democracy hangs in the balance.

— Wiley

RALEIGH NEWS & OBSERVER
Former US Rep: Democrats must match Republican gerrymandering — for now, at least. [https://www.newsobserver.com/opinion/article311747810.html]
Opinion | Wiley Nickel
In 2023, I was gerrymandered out of my seat in Congress. The three House seats we lost in North Carolina were ultimately the difference in control of the 119th Congress. If our North Carolina maps had been fair, Rep. Hakeem Jeffries would be the Speaker of the House with a razor thin 218-217 majority and things would look very different in Washington. Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill would have been dead on arrival and Democrats would have protected America from another round of billionaire tax giveaways and major cuts to Medicaid.
A fair system created by non-partisan independent redistricting is the only way for our democracy to survive.
The mid-decade redistricting by Republicans in Texas is not normal. If Texas is allowed to flip five seats from Democrat to Republican with the stroke of a pen it’s very likely going to decide control of the next Congress. Democrats must respond in-kind to any mid-decade partisan gerrymandering.
While it’s necessary now for Democratic states to match the conniving of Republicans for any semblance of a fair House of Representatives to remain, this tit-for-tat redistricting must end, and our country must move toward a totally different way of drawing voting maps.
There is a right way to run a democracy. Look at what happened in North Carolina.
In 2022, the North Carolina Supreme Court took up the job of drawing congressional districts for our state after NC Republicans proved unwilling to draw fair maps. The court drew a fair map with seven Republican seats, six Democratic seats plus one GOP-leaning district that could have gone either way. I won that 50/50 seat by three points and went to Washington to solve problems and work across the aisle like my constituents — Republicans, Democrats and Independents — elected me to do.
That’s how Democracy should look — a playing field that’s fair and competitive.
Unfortunately in that same year, Democrats lost our majority on the NC Supreme Court, and Republicans in our state legislature quickly proceeded to hack our democracy with zero checks from the new GOP majority on our court. Republicans gerrymandered our state once again, and flipped three seats from blue to red before a single voter had a chance to cast a ballot. Those three seats were just enough to give Trump control of Congress.
To fix the chaos and confusion in Washington we need more districts that look exactly like the old 13th District that sent me to Washington.
Sadly less than 10% of all congressional districts fall into that category. Thanks to gerrymandering we really only have about 40 out of 435 House seats that could flip in any given election.
In Congress, I authored the Fair Maps Act. It’s the best solution we have at the federal level. It would force all states to play by the same set of rules by requiring non-partisan independent redistricting commissions in every state. The experts at Duke told us that if my bill became law we’d go from around 40 to over 80 competitive seats in Congress. That would be a game changer. Plus the mid-decade maps they cooked up in Texas would clearly be illegal.
Most folks in Washington expect that Democrats will flip the House in the 2026 midterms. Trump’s only hope to keep control of Congress is to cheat. Gerrymandering is brutally effective, and he’s looking to flip as many seats as possible in Texas and other states by drawing new lines rather than by convincing moderate voters that Republicans actually have better ideas.
Republican extremists don’t want to do the work of fairly winning voters. They’d rather cheat through gerrymandering, and frankly they’ve done a much better job at it. Democrats have been totally screwed by gerrymandering over the past decade. When you count up all the states where extreme partisan gerrymandering exists, you’ll find some districts where Democrats gerrymander, but many more where Republicans gerrymander. The non-partisan Brennan Center has done the math and concluded that Republicans have gained a 16 seat gerrymandering advantage nationwide. If Texas has its way, that number goes above 20 seats. After the 2030 census, when red states like Texas and Florida gain even more seats in Congress, we could expect the Republican gerrymandering advantage to go above 30 seats. That would all but guarantee that Democrats have no real path to a majority ever again with such an uneven playing field.
There is hope on this issue. We can forever put an end to this mess with legislation forcing the states to draw fair congressional maps. Voters should trust Democrats to deliver on this issue simply because the current system of extreme partisan gerrymandering heavily favors Republicans nationwide.
Blue states will need to match Republican gerrymandering, for the time being, in order to preserve our democracy. If Democrats fail to respond to GOP gerrymandering in Texas then partisan map makers, not voters, will likely hand control of Congress to Trump once again.
Former Congressman Wiley Nickel represented North Carolina’s 13th Congressional District in the U.S. House of Representatives from 2023 to 2025.

HERE'S WHAT'S NEXT:
Democrats have a lot of work ahead of us. We need to organize against Republican gerrymandering in Texas — and we need to get ready to win big in 2026.

Please like and share my message on social media to get the message out. [https://x.com/wileynickel/status/1957833632942321742]

https://x.com/wileynickel/status/1957833632942321742 [https://x.com/wileynickel/status/1957833632942321742]

Raleigh, NC 27602
United States

name: Voter Action Project
subject: A chilling headline
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Slate: The Supreme Court Is Determined to Turn Voting Into a Limited Privilege [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=081925_3&amount=25]
At Voter Action Project, Team, we believe that voting is a fundamental right , not a privilege or some test of everyone's endurance and ability to wait in a long line. We believe that our right to vote is the core bedrock that makes up our democracy and needs to be defended, expanded, and celebrated.
However, as the Voting Rights Act -- a historic piece of legislation that stood for voting as a fundamental right for every American -- recently turned 60 years old, it is being eviscerated to stop voters and their lawyers from defending that freedom to vote in court.
Conservatives on the United States Supreme Court have spent the last two decades slowly tearing the VRA to pieces. In the hands of opponents of voting rights, the Voting Rights Act has faced death by a thousand cuts. Some of those cuts have been small, such as limiting how courts consider challenges brought under the law. And some have been large, as in the 2013 decision in Shelby County v. Holder that ended the requirement for certain states with histories of discrimination to submit election changes and district maps to the Department of Justice for approval.
As the Supreme Court holds over another case, hell-bent on dismantling the Voting Rights Act piece by piece, we have to fight to protect our fundamental rights.
Pitch in $25 or whatever you can today to stand up for the VRA and help us protect voting rights nationwide >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=081925_3&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=081925_3&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=081925_3&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=081925_3&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=081925_3&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=081925_3&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=081925_3&amount=35]
Doubt us? The U.S. Supreme Court is now set to hear oral argument in Callais v. Landry — the high-stakes Louisiana redistricting case that could upend the Voting Rights Act — on October 15.
This will be the second time the nation’s highest court hears oral argument in the pivotal voting rights case. This fall, the argument will focus on whether Louisiana’s creation of a second majority-minorty district under the VRA violates the U.S. Constitution.
This is a key opportunity for Chief Justice John Roberts, who has taken every opportunity since being appointed to the court by President George W. Bush to destroy the VRA. This case could take out one of the last legs holding the law up in favor of equality and our fundamental right to have our voice heard in an election.
Over the last two decades, as the Supreme Court dismantled the VRA case by case, voter suppression has evolved. It’s been built on paperwork, confusion, inconvenience, and ever-changing rules. That’s not how it should be. We need to put justice back in reach for those who need it most.
Please, fuel our work to stop them with $25 or whatever you can today and defend our fundamental right to vote >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=081925_3&amount=25]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=081925_3&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=081925_3&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.

name: StateDemocrats.com
subject: Another U.S. court just confirmed that DOGE can access Americans’ sensitive personal information
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
Do YOU oppose DOGE accessing your personal data?
#

Earlier this month, another U.S. court confirmed that Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency — otherwise known as **DOGE — can legally access the personal data of MILLIONS of Americans like you.** **This includes _sensitive information._** Social Security numbers. Phone numbers. Health records. Employment records. Addresses. We think most Americans would agree on this... **DOGE shouldn’t be anywhere near our private information.** But after this ruling, we want to confirm with you, Peter: **Do YOU oppose DOGE accessing your personal data?** **[YES — I'M OPPOSED](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93929?t=1&akid=9295%2E614464%2EN6G-wG)** **[NO — I SUPPORT IT](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93929?t=2&akid=9295%2E614464%2EN6G-wG)** **[I'M NOT SURE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93929?t=3&akid=9295%2E614464%2EN6G-wG)**
---

name: Nancy Mace for Governor
subject: I dropped out of high school. Then I became a Congresswoman. Now, God has other plans for me…

**High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.** **Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.** At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts. **By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch.** She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground.Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina. Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks. **She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.** Nancy is fighting to:
* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast
* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency
* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free
* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate
* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think
* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. **South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.**
---
| |

At 17, I dropped out of high school. My parents, a tough Army General and an even tougher schoolteacher, gave me two choices: Go back to school, or get a job. So I went to work at a fine-dining establishment some of you may know as Waffle House. Simply put, I’ve had to work for everything I have ever had in life. Jesus carried me through trauma, heartbreak, single motherhood, public failure, and private grief. He carried me**when I was diagnosed with PTSD. He carried me when I moved four times to keep my children safe. And when I had nothing left to give,He filled me with living water. Twenty-five years ago, I made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel. I got here the hard way. And I’ve been FIGHTING ever since: 💥 I go toe-to-toe with Fake News CNN anchors on live TV.💥 I fight every single day for President Trump’s agenda. 💥 I’ve suffered DEATH THREATS for fighting to keep MEN out of women’s bathrooms. This isn’t about headlines. I’ve had a target on my back since I took on the establishment, but now they’re serious. They will stop at nothing to silence me. Dems are hurling MILLIONS against me, and establishment Republicans are throwing their support behind RINOs just to shut me down. The battle is just getting started. I can’t do this without you. Every dollar fuels our movement, whether it’s spreading new yard signs across the state or buying pizza and water for my grassroots volunteers. I wasn’t born with a silver spoon in my mouth. name: Chris Ahuja
subject: Putin’s playbook has no place in America
body:
The Voting Rights Act is under attack. We will fight to defend it.

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=43f21206d9&e=5a6ee82cdb)
| | | | | [Donate](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=4d2c298db5&e=5a6ee82cdb)
---
| | Dear Peter,**Sixty years ago, brave Americans marched, risked everything, and even shed blood in Selma so the Voting Rights Act could become law.** They fought so every American—no matter their race, zip code, or income—could have a voice at the ballot box.**Today, Donald Trump wants to tear that progress apart.** Trump bragged recently about taking advice from Vladimir Putin, a dictator, who told him to eliminate mail-in voting to secure elections. **Let’s be clear: this is not security. It is suppression, pure and simple.****Mail-in voting has opened doors for millions of people**. Seniors, working parents, disabled Americans, and communities that have too often been shut out now have access to the ballot. Trump’s plan would slam those doors shut and drag us back to a darker time.**I refuse to let that happen.** I will fight with everything I have to protect the right to vote for every American in every community. But I cannot do it alone.If you believe our democracy is worth fighting for, I am asking you to stand with me today. [**Chip in $15, $25, or whatever you can to help us build a campaign strong enough to stop Trump’s plan.**](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=fab52bead5&e=5a6ee82cdb)**The Voting Rights Act was won through courage and sacrifice. Honoring that legacy means fighting back with the same determination.** Together, we can protect our democracy and make sure every voice is heard.**In solidarity,****
**[****](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=49c69c89f2&e=5a6ee82cdb)**Chris Ahuja**
**Democrat for Congress, CD-32**

---

name: Kathryn Harvey
subject: My race starts tonight.
body:
Join me September 8.

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/a016acbe36ff/kathryn-harvey-for-congresscampaign-kickoff-6421005?e=edbde6c9b1)

|

|
---
| | Welcome to Keeping Up with Kathryn Harvey. A weekly digest covering South Carolina politics and opportunities for engagement and action in the Upstate.
---
| |
### My race starts tonight!
---
[|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=bd7a910591&e=edbde6c9b1)
| |
### Tonight my race for Spartanburg County Council has officially begun. I’m running to be a councilperson for everyone, whether you’re on the Eastside of Spartanburg, Cowpens, Pacolet or Mayo. It’s up the voters to decide what they want their leadership to look like, and I’m committed to showing up for this community in every way I can. You can get me to the finish line by signing up to [knock doors](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=234a0f9564&e=edbde6c9b1), [calling voters](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=1efb68c7e4&e=edbde6c9b1), and [contributing now ](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=facbcf0625&e=edbde6c9b1)to help me reach my end of month deadline.
---
| [Contribute to Kathryn](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=b7cc15cd43&e=edbde6c9b1)

# Knock doors with me on Saturdays & Sundays
---

---
|

| | | | | |
# Phone bank training Thursday
---
| | Interested in phone banking for my campaign this Fall? Join us Thursday evening for a virtual phone bank training! This is a great opportunity to volunteer on your own time and help my race for County Council. Register at the link below and you'll receive a zoom link or can join in person at 505 East Main Street.
---
| [Register Here](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=4874b51e72&e=edbde6c9b1)
---
|
---
|
---
| | | |
# **Meet me in Greenville August 26 **
---
| Join me, Councilwoman Lillian Brock Flemming, and Dr. Annie Andrews on August 26 at the Hughes Main Library in Greenville for the monthly GCDP meeting. Learn how you can get involved in building a stronger effort to elect more women up and down the ballot in the Upstate.
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=729c674007&e=edbde6c9b1)
| |
# SPCDP Monthly Meeting September 8
---
| | **Join us at the Spartanburg Community College Downtown Campus Theater at 220 East Kennedy Street on September 8 for our monthly meeting in our news space** as we hear from a panel of elected officials and strategists around the state on how we can strengthen our party. The remainder of our 2025 meetings will be held at this location September 8 | October 6 | November 10 | December 1.
---
[|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=a7e9ae431e&e=edbde6c9b1)
|
---
| |
# SC Sister Senators at Wofford Sept 29
---
| | Join us for the second installment of “Candid Conversations” an effort to bring together leadership from across the isle to foster civil discourse in today’s political climate. Hosted by the President’s Office at Wofford College, the SC Sister Senators who bridged together to fight for women’s rights in South Carolina, and were awarded the JFK Profile in Courage Award for their efforts, will join us for a special panel session and screening of their new documentary film on September 29 at 6pm at Wofford.
---
|
---
| |
****Canvass with Kathryn Harvey every Sunday at 3pm**[**RSVP Here**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=9e0031b40c&e=edbde6c9b1)****** **August 21: Phone Bank Training (Virtual or In Person)**[**RSVP Here**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=acb8ee5282&e=edbde6c9b1)**August 26: GCDP monthly meeting at Hughes Main Library - Greenville****September 8: SPCDP Monthly meeting at SCC Downtown Campus****September 29: Evening with SC Sister Senators at Wofford College** October 21 - 31 Early Voting for the 2025 General Election 8:30 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. daily (Closed Saturday and Sunday), County Administration Building.**November 4: 2025 General Election (7:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m.)**

I’m running to be a councilperson for everyone, whether you’re on the Eastside of Spartanburg, Cowpens, Pacolet or Mayo. It’s up the voters to decide what they want their leadership to look like, and I’m committed to showing up for this community in every way I can. You can get me to the finish line by signing up to , , and help me reach my end of month deadline. | | | | Knock doors with me on Saturdays & Sundays | | Join me every Saturday at 10am & Sunday at 3pm for a neighborhood canvass to support my race for Spartanburg County Council! * * *
* **TOP NEWS**

**" Texas Democrats end walkout over redistricting as California prepares to retaliate," **AP's**Bill Barrow** and**Fernanda Figueroa** \-- "Texas Democrats ended a two-week walkout Monday that stalled Republican efforts to redraw congressional districts as part of [a national partisan brawl](https://substack.com/redirect/2ccd2247-3ae9-4ea0-a44b-a4205baec0e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) over President **Donald Trump 's** desire to reshape U.S. House maps to his advantage.

Their return to the Texas Capitol -- which will now involve escorts by state police to make sure the lawmakers don't bolt again -- puts the Republican-run Legislature in position to satisfy Trump's demands as California Democrats separately [advance new congressional boundaries](https://substack.com/redirect/faeb7848-c1bc-4410-b2ff-24140f522bf1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in retaliation. The tit-for-tat puts the nation's two most populous states at the center of an expanding fight over [control of Congress](https://substack.com/redirect/be79fb3f-0a09-4091-971f-440a0a79f575?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ahead of the 2026 midterm elections. The battle also has [rallied Democrats nationally](https://substack.com/redirect/af85e735-a3f7-4f52-81f8-31b40aaea727?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after infighting and frustrations among the party's own voters since Republicans took control of the White House and Capitol Hill in January.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/62957f35-2c7e-4bf9-abe4-e6139bd549a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In Texas, dozens of state House Democrats left the state Aug. 3 to deny their Republican-majority colleagues the attendance necessary to vote on redrawn maps intended to send five more Texas Republicans to Washington. After spending nearly two weeks in Illinois and elsewhere, they declared victory when Republicans adjourned their first special session on Friday and Democrats around the country rallied in opposition to the Trump-led gerrymandering effort. They pointed specifically to [California's release of proposed maps](https://substack.com/redirect/96e9f0aa-a883-40e3-92c3-47f9c4d4f208?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) intended to increase Democrats' U.S. House advantage by five seats, effectively neutralizing any Republican gains in Texas.

Many of the absent Democrats left Chicago early Monday and landed hours later at a private airfield in Austin, where several boarded a large charter bus to the Capitol. Once inside, they were greeted by cheering supporters. And for the first time since Trump's redistricting push accelerated into a national issue, the Texas House floor was near full capacity when lawmakers convened briefly Monday afternoon.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/62957f35-2c7e-4bf9-abe4-e6139bd549a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Republican House Speaker **Dustin Burrows** did not mention redistricting on the floor Monday but promised swift action on the Legislature's agenda.

"The majority has the right to prevail. The minority has the right to be heard," the speaker said. "We are done waiting."

Burrows had previously issued civil arrest warrants for the absent lawmakers under House rules, and his office said Monday that Texas Department of Public Safety officers will follow the previously departed lawmakers around the clock to ensure that they return again.

Cheering supporters greeted returning lawmakers inside the Capitol before the House convened for a brief session.

"We killed the corrupt special session, withstood unprecedented surveillance and intimidation, and rallied Democrats nationwide to join this existential fight for fair representation -- reshaping the entire 2026 landscape," Texas House Minority Leader**Gene Wu** said in a written statement.

Wu has promised Democrats would challenge the new designs in court.

The House did not take up any bills Monday and was not scheduled to return until Wednesday." [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/2f03a851-982e-4923-9fe5-444861288319?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**" Returning Texas House Democrats put on around-the-clock surveillance," **Houston Chronicle's**Taylor Goldenstein** , **Bayliss Wagner** and **John C. Moritz** \-- "The Texas House is assigning state troopers to monitor Democratic lawmakers who returned to the Capitol on Monday, an extraordinary step as GOP leaders seek to prevent another disruption in their push to redraw the state's congressional maps.

From the dais, House Speaker **Dustin Burrows** on Monday said he would not allow the Democrats to leave the chamber unless they agreed to be released into the custody of a designated Department of Public Safety escort, who will "ensure your return Wednesday" when the body meets next.

He noted that civil arrest warrants were still in place for the handful of Democrats who did not return on Monday from their two-week walkout over the redistricting plan.

The escorts, whom a House official confirmed would be watching "around the clock," represent a major escalation in that effort and test the limits of what can be done to enforce a House rule and civil warrant not directly linked to any state or federal law.

After the House adjourned Monday, dozens of Democrats lined up in a hallway behind the chamber to sign permission slips.

Members were each paired with plainclothes DPS agents. There was a levity to the scene as members and their escorts became acquainted, neither seeming totally clear on the details of the situation.

After a Hearst Newspapers reporter asked who his designated escort was, state Rep. **John Bryant** said, "I don't know."

"I am," said the man standing next to him.

Bryant asked the trooper if he was coming home to Dallas with him.

"They told me I have to stay glued to your hip," the trooper, who declined to provide his full name, said.

Republican leaders have been working to bring down the hammer on Democrats who fled the state earlier this month to deny the chamber a quorum.

In addition to new $500-per-day fines on members who joined the exodus, Burrows had already moved to cut members' office budgets and force members to pick up paychecks in person, rather than through electronic payment. Attorney General **Ken Paxton** filed suits trying to force out-of-state authorities to coordinate with Texas to bring them home (thus far unsuccessful), and Gov. **Greg Abbott** has filed suit to have House Democratic Caucus Chairman **Gene Wu** , of Houston, removed from office." [Hou Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/d3d920b9-1659-48d2-816d-f59fc7eb7427?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

TOP NEWS " Texas Democrats end walkout over redistricting as California prepares to retaliate," AP'sBill Barrow andFernanda Figueroa \-- "Texas Democrats ended a two-week walkout Monday that stalled Republican efforts to redraw congressional districts as part of over President Donald Trump 's desire to reshape U.S. House maps to his advantage. Their return to the Texas Capitol -- which will now involve escorts by state police to make sure the lawmakers don't bolt again -- puts the Republican-run Legislature in position to satisfy Trump's demands as California Democrats separately in retaliation. The tit-for-tat puts the nation's two most populous states at the center of an expanding fight over ahead of the 2026 midterm elections. The battle also has after infighting and frustrations among the party's own voters since Republicans took control of the White House and Capitol Hill in January. In Texas, dozens of state House Democrats left the state Aug. 3 to deny their Republican-majority colleagues the attendance necessary to vote on redrawn maps intended to send five more Texas Republicans to Washington. After spending nearly two weeks in Illinois and elsewhere, they declared victory when Republicans adjourned their first special session on Friday and Democrats around the country rallied in opposition to the Trump-led gerrymandering effort. They pointed specifically to intended to increase Democrats' U.S. House advantage by five seats, effectively neutralizing any Republican gains in Texas. Many of the absent Democrats left Chicago early Monday and landed hours later at a private airfield in Austin, where several boarded a large charter bus to the Capitol. Once inside, they were greeted by cheering supporters. And for the first time since Trump's redistricting push accelerated into a national issue, the Texas House floor was near full capacity when lawmakers convened briefly Monday afternoon. Republican House Speaker Dustin Burrows did not mention redistricting on the floor Monday but promised swift action on the Legislature's agenda. "The majority has the right to prevail. The minority has the right to be heard," the speaker said. "We are done waiting." Burrows had previously issued civil arrest warrants for the absent lawmakers under House rules, and his office said Monday that Texas Department of Public Safety officers will follow the previously departed lawmakers around the clock to ensure that they return again. Cheering supporters greeted returning lawmakers inside the Capitol before the House convened for a brief session. "We killed the corrupt special session, withstood unprecedented surveillance and intimidation, and rallied Democrats nationwide to join this existential fight for fair representation -- reshaping the entire 2026 landscape," Texas House Minority LeaderGene Wu said in a written statement. Wu has promised Democrats would challenge the new designs in court. The House did not take up any bills Monday and was not scheduled to return until Wednesday." " Returning Texas House Democrats put on around-the-clock surveillance," Houston Chronicle'sTaylor Goldenstein , Bayliss Wagner and John C. Moritz \-- "The Texas House is assigning state troopers to monitor Democratic lawmakers who returned to the Capitol on Monday, an extraordinary step as GOP leaders seek to prevent another disruption in their push to redraw the state's congressional maps. From the dais, House Speaker Dustin Burrows on Monday said he would not allow the Democrats to leave the chamber unless they agreed to be released into the custody of a designated Department of Public Safety escort, who will "ensure your return Wednesday" when the body meets next. He noted that civil arrest warrants were still in place for the handful of Democrats who did not return on Monday from their two-week walkout over the redistricting plan. The escorts, whom a House official confirmed would be watching "around the clock," represent a major escalation in that effort and test the limits of what can be done to enforce a House rule and civil warrant not directly linked to any state or federal law. After the House adjourned Monday, dozens of Democrats lined up in a hallway behind the chamber to sign permission slips. Members were each paired with plainclothes DPS agents. There was a levity to the scene as members and their escorts became acquainted, neither seeming totally clear on the details of the situation. After a Hearst Newspapers reporter asked who his designated escort was, state Rep. John Bryant said, "I don't know." "I am," said the man standing next to him. Bryant asked the trooper if he was coming home to Dallas with him. "They told me I have to stay glued to your hip," the trooper, who declined to provide his full name, said. Republican leaders have been working to bring down the hammer on Democrats who fled the state earlier this month to deny the chamber a quorum. In addition to new $500-per-day fines on members who joined the exodus, Burrows had already moved to cut members' office budgets and force members to pick up paychecks in person, rather than through electronic payment. Attorney General Ken Paxton filed suits trying to force out-of-state authorities to coordinate with Texas to bring them home (thus far unsuccessful), and Gov. Greg Abbott has filed suit to have House Democratic Caucus Chairman Gene Wu , of Houston, removed from office." * **TOP NEWS**

**" Texas House set to vote on redistricting maps Wednesday," **Dallas Morning News'**Philip Jankowski** \-- "The Texas House is set to vote on congressional redistricting Wednesday, setting up a political fight over a plan that could be critical for Republicans to hold on to a majority in the 2026 midterm elections.

The House is rushing to approve the plan after enough Democrats returned to the Capitol to restore a constitutionally required quorum. Work had been halted since Aug. 4 in the chamber after more than 50 House Democrats left the state to block a vote on the bill.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/fd5b463f-7130-4620-81cc-e2edacfabf5b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The redistricting plan, which advanced out of a committee late Tuesday, reshapes urban districts in North Texas, Houston, San Antonio and Austin. Several districts that have heavily favored Democrats would be transformed into districts expected to vote for Republicans by wide margins.

In North Texas, the plan aimed at Democratic U.S. House Reps.**Marc Veasey a** nd**Julie Johnson**. Veasey was moved out of his district while Johnson's district was stretched to include a wide swath of northeast Texas, turning it from a safe Democratic district to one that would have voted for President **Donald Trump** by 41 percentage points.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/fd5b463f-7130-4620-81cc-e2edacfabf5b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
House lawmakers redrawing the maps are led by Rep. **Todd Hunter** , R-Corpus Christi. Hunter's latest iteration of the map made public Monday makes further alterations to the Houston area.

There, Republicans are set to gain one seat in Congress by transforming the district represented by Democrat Rep**. Al Green** from an urban southern Houston district that favored former Vice President **Kamala Harris** in the 2024 election by 44 percentage points to a district that combines shards of eastern Houston with a swath of rural east Texas that stretches 60 miles to the north and east.

The new district would have voted for Trump over Harris by nearly 20 percentage points. Compared to a previous draft plan, it pushes what was the most drastic change in the partisan makeup of a district even further to the right.

Trump has called for Texas to flip five seats to the GOP. If Democrats gain control of the U.S. House, it would likely kneecap his ability to enact his agenda in the final two years of his term. Congressional Democrats would also gain the ability to launch investigations into his administration or possibly impeach Trump again.

On Tuesday, Trump praised the redistricting effort on social media, thanking Gov. **Greg Abbott** , Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** and House Speaker **Dustin Burrows** , R-Lubbock.

"It is wonderful to see Republicans everywhere standing up to Save our Country. It is one of the most popular initiatives I have ever supported. Republicans love watching us fight for a Great Cause. Please pass this Map, ASAP," Trump said." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/99db4768-42a1-4759-afb0-d1404cfd0972?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

* **STATE GOVERNMENT**

**" Appeals court overturns order that stripped some protections from pregnant Texas state workers" **[AP](https://substack.com/redirect/4f2738ea-3b7c-43bb-989e-563dafba0043?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**" Texas prison agency names senior official as its next chief," **AP's **Alex Nguyen** \-- "**Bobby Lumpkin** will be the Texas Department of Criminal Justice's next executive director, leading one of the largest state agencies that is also in the midst of a correctional staffing crisis.

Lumpkin, who is currently the agency's chief operations officer, will assume the top role on Sept. 1, TDCJ's governing board announced Tuesday. He replaces Executive Director **Bryan Collier** , who is set to retire on Aug. 31 after nine years on the job.

Lumpkin, who [joined TDCJ in 1990](https://substack.com/redirect/ea0d439b-4747-4044-9bc3-1c33f4211616?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as a correctional officer, will soon head an agency that oversees over 100 prisons and around 140,000 inmates.

This work is taking place amid a statewide [staffing shortage](https://substack.com/redirect/48d3d4e5-4fba-4755-a7a0-deaca8f3e272?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), including high vacancy and turnover rates that could pose risks to employees and inmates, according to [a 2024 report](https://substack.com/redirect/09bd9d6c-437a-429c-91e0-395f56ecb0ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the Texas Sunset Advisory Commission. The state Legislature has recently [approved funding](https://substack.com/redirect/35c851dc-142b-4202-9c0d-3e2dc54caaeb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for TDCJ that would go toward pay raises, targeted salary adjustments and other initiatives to address staffing.

He will also have to continue addressing the lack of air conditioning in prisons, which a federal judge has ruled earlier this year as [unconstitutional](https://substack.com/redirect/566234ac-78d8-42b0-9f25-75aa4624f4c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and pushed for a trial. TDCJ is [working on](https://substack.com/redirect/5fb3f221-5a47-4a0d-88a4-ba730bdce8a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) installing cool beds, but the majority of Texas prisons are still not fully air conditioned. The staffing shortage also [contributes](https://substack.com/redirect/bb087ede-8db0-4c17-bb9c-18875ad6e454?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the challenge.

A trial over the issue is currently scheduled to take place next year.

At the same time, Lumpkin will take the reins of an agency that is undergoing a [shift in focus towards rehabilitation](https://substack.com/redirect/40e458f5-d37a-4030-8ff5-a0ac0b14a316?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), as outlined in its "2030 Vision" plan.

Collier [announced](https://substack.com/redirect/40a0ce6a-b038-4ba0-9872-79c8141ac10d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) his intention to retire in June. He first joined the agency as a clerk in 1985 and rose through its ranks over four decades, before being named its leader in 2016. On Tuesday, the TDCJ's governing board also named [a new training facility](https://substack.com/redirect/0189f728-4c54-424d-a614-4ec4bc7b59c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Huntsville after him.

As of July, Collier's [annual salary](https://substack.com/redirect/869bc890-cda2-4b8c-814c-fb8f8522c85f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as executive director was nearly $300,000." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/02d30126-eeb0-48e9-851b-d44aa496f519?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **LOCAL GOVERNMENT**

**" No employee benefit cuts, but Harris County targets departments for reductions amid $48M budget deficit," **Houston Chronicle's **John Lomax V** \-- "Harris County's deficit remains at roughly $48 million [following a decision Thursday not to cut employee benefits or pension contributions.](https://substack.com/redirect/3b8c513a-b567-4a34-8f4c-dc2cbb90849d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

The county's $2.7 billion budget will likely need to be balanced by cuts to some of the county's major departments, including Harris County Public Health, Engineering and Housing and Community Development, according to an internal email shared with the Houston Chronicle.

It's the latest move in a game of financial Jenga that's driven a rift between the court's four Democrats. Judge**Lina Hidalgo** and Commissioner **Rodney Ellis** have voiced concerns that the proposed budget would slash crucial services, while Commissioners **Adrian Garcia** and **Lesley Briones** advocated cutting vacant positions and implementing common-sense efficiencies that they allege would have a negligible impact on county-run programs.

"We all know we have a deficit, and what we have done is lean in to being solutions-oriented -- finding ways we could do more with less, finding those savings," Briones said during a news conference held Thursday morning. "Some would like you to believe that the sky has fallen, that there will be devastating cuts in this dystopian universe, false."

While Garcia and Briones lobbed vague accusations of unnamed actors seeking to spread "false narratives," Hidalgo told reporters at a separate news conference just 30 minutes later that Briones and Garcia -- [two parts of what she later dubbed the "GOP 3"](https://substack.com/redirect/e7bbc61e-791e-47b9-98b6-c0263318d013?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) along with Republican Commissioner**Tom Ramse** y -- were attempting to steer the narrative in their favor.

Hidalgo accused the pair of obfuscating the impact of a previous vote to provide sweeping raises to county law enforcement officers. The raises were intended to prevent an exodus of deputies to the Houston Police Department, which triggered a domino effect following the city's approval of a [$1 billion police contract that included a 36.5% raise for officers spread](https://substack.com/redirect/1143e7f2-d861-4221-a0e9-62946c9b2d23?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across five years.

"Our hands are tied because my colleagues decided not to go to the voters on this $108 million increase for law enforcement that we couldn't afford," Hidalgo said. "Not only are elected leaders putting your county in the hole, but they are refusing to ask you whether you would like to see cuts. We are forcing you to have cuts."

Although the raises were initially projected to cost the county roughly $140 million, that estimate proved to be an additional $30 million more than the actual cost." [Hou Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/f97c5204-7b54-46db-b81b-409dfb44b2f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

**" Mayor Jones ramps up public pressure to delay Spurs arena deal," **San Antonio Express-News' **Devyani Chhetri** \-- "Mayor **Gina Ortiz Jones** isn't here to make friends.

She tried to upstage Councilman **Marc Whyte** -- one of her main foils on City Council -- at his Northeast Side district's budget town hall on Monday and turn his residents against him over his support for approving a deal with the Spurs over a downtown arena.

Jones is calling on the City Council on Thursday [to approve a resolution she crafted](https://substack.com/redirect/fad73d5c-d101-44ad-bf83-9a6bef8e61d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that would halt a vote on a proposed term sheet with Spurs Sports & Entertainment. Before the council weighs in, she wants an independent economic impact report on the arena and for each council member to hold two town halls in their district to gather constituents' opinions on the study and the proposed deal.

"The Spurs want (the arena) to cost $1.3 billion. You know what you would be on the hook for? $489 million," Jones told residents who showed up to the Northeast Senior Center to talk about their spending priorities for the next city budget.

"I think you deserve an opportunity to review the data, review the draft term sheet before your councilman votes on it," she continued. "And if you agree with me, please encourage him to support my resolution on Thursday."

Whyte is one of five council members who want the council to -- instead of going along with Jones' resolution -- approve a dueling resolution Thursday that would authorize City Manager **Erik Walsh** to execute [the proposed term sheet with the franchise](https://substack.com/redirect/08368c44-2fea-49fe-9d09-05ffa75b50b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

The District 10 councilman believes the terms must be finalized before Bexar County voters [go to the polls on Nov. 4](https://substack.com/redirect/84407019-8925-4dc2-976e-f037248c6115?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to decide whether to increase the county hotel occupancy tax to raise $311 million to help build an up to 18,500-seat NBA at Hemisfair.

The Spurs would kick in $500 million and pay for any cost overruns, according to the proposed terms, which were made public Monday.

The city's $489M portion would be financed with bond debt that would be repaid by ground and facility leases with SS&E, property tax revenue from surrounding development guaranteed by the franchise and state hotel tax revenue. The city's contribution does not include the roughly $60 million the city expects to pay to acquire the former Institute of Texan Cultures site.

The arena would be the centerpiece of a larger sports and entertainment district proposed by city staff known as Project Marvel.

Whyte was visibly frustrated by Jones' remarks. He told attendees that "the dishonesty unfortunately continues" because the mayor's chief of staff told him Jones would exclusively speak about the budget.

"We had an agreement literally five minutes ago that we would allow the mayor to come up and speak if we kept it to the budget and not on Project Marvel -- and yet we just saw what took place," Whyte said.

That prompted Jones to return to the front of the room. She wagged her finger "no," made faces and laughed while he spoke, and at times attempted to talk over him.

It was par for the course in how the two have interacted since she took office June 18. She has publicly admonished him for standing up before she has adjourned a meeting. And they frequently interrupt one another at meetings, such as when she grilled him earlier this month over how the deal's numbers would add up.

The District 10 budget town hall coincided with one in District 3. Jones told the San Antonio Express-News that she plans to attend [as many as she can](https://substack.com/redirect/88a5d27d-a87d-40f0-8128-3ac9d5d92090?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) over the next two weeks.

Whyte told the newspaper that the mayor's behavior was a continuation of her "PR campaign against moving forward with the term sheet."

Hours earlier, Jones gathered community organizers who oppose spending public dollars on Project Marvel to [stand beside her on the steps of City Hall](https://substack.com/redirect/946643b4-0dfa-4eb5-b5c7-34d44e1f3917?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as she made her pitch for why council members should pass her resolution -- rather than the one OKing the terms of the deal. She characterized council members who want to rush the deal as not "serious about representing their residents."

"I'm very shocked that folks would want to move forward on this absent from hearing from their constituents," Jones said at her press conference.

Jones dodged reporters' questions on whether she has the votes to pass her resolution. She needs the support of at least five council members.

Asked how the votes on the competing resolutions would play out Thursday, she simply said, "We'll see."

However, beyond ramping up public pressure on the council, it appears that she has done little privately to persuade those in favor of approving the terms to come to her side.

"I don't know who she has talked to," Whyte told the Express-News. "Her style thus far has simply been to issue memos that show up in our inbox."

The strained relationship between Jones and some of the council was on display at his budget town hall. Whyte invited Councilwomen **Teri Castillo** and **Marina Alderete Gavito** , who represent the city's West and near Northwest Sides, respectively, to the event.

The trio teamed up last month to force Jones to hold a meeting to debate changes she made to how council members can propose new policies, programs and municipal laws -- changes that [she walked back last week](https://substack.com/redirect/0dfc79e4-a89a-4120-b6f1-34ee1aee020c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after it became clear that she lacked the support to have them followed.

It was a short-lived win for the three. The next day, Jones passed over each of them when she [named the chairs of her new City Council Committees](https://substack.com/redirect/66c63f69-b355-4e91-a871-4b236d73de60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a coveted position that former mayors have given to more-senior council members.

Alderete Gavito and Castillo sang Whyte's praises before his residents, even though the progressive Castillo is often at odds with the conservative councilman. They also spoke about the need to work together at City Hall.

"Being on council is a team sport, and together we're getting things done for y'all," Alderete Gavito said.

"It's about collaborating, working with each other to ensure we're well aware of what those needs are throughout the city of San Antonio," Castillo said." [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/e1d34fb3-a29a-4406-ba19-45c9b064c646?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

* **2026**

**" Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick launches campaign for reelection," **San Antonio Current's **Sanford Nowlin** \-- "Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** on Tuesday launched his campaign for reelection, vowing to continue to push conservative, Christian-based legislation that protects children and taxpayers.

"I'm not stepping back at any point in time," Patrick, 75, said during a news conference at The Austin Club before joining supporters for a campaign kickoff reception. "I'm all in. I've always been all in."

First elected in 2014, Patrick is seeking his fourth term as lieutenant governor. As the presiding officer of the Senate, he touches every piece of major legislation, and most analysts contend he's been the most powerful legislative force in Texas.

Already [endorsed](https://substack.com/redirect/9b09b9bf-2c10-42c2-868b-eb2dc6673614?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by President **Donald Trump** , Patrick on Tuesday touted the conservative legislation approved during his tenure, including property tax relief, strict anti-abortion laws, education reform and laws that protect children.

Standing with him were at least seven senators up for reelection, as well as three candidates who he's endorsed.

"I don't get the credit," Patrick said of his accomplishments. "I just get the privilege to lead them."

Patrick also addressed rumors he wouldn't seek reelection, even as he's previously insisted he's running next year for another four-year term.

"I didn't raise $5 million in five days in June if I wasn't running," he said. "I didn't accumulate, which will be $40 to $45 million in our bank account not to run."

Patrick said news about his "campaign announcement" created more speculation. He described a text message he received from a friend who runs a Bible mission in Hanoi, Vietnam.

"I hear you're not running," the message said. "I hear that you're going to be the senior pastor of Second Baptist Church."

Patrick said there have been all kinds of rumors about his future.

"I hear that maybe there was a health issue. I don't have any health issues, folks. I'm in great health," Patrick said.

"Tell all those would-be opponents out there Dan's in. He's in it to win it as always," Patrick said. "We're going to be victorious in the primary. We're going to be victorious in the general, and we're just going to make Texas even more prosperous and safer and better in the future."

Patrick does not have a major primary challenger. Democrat **Vikki Goodwi** n, D-Dallas, is planning to challenge him in the general election.

Patrick said he would use another term to continue to bring Texans property tax relief.

"We need to continue to reduce property taxes," Patrick said. "We've come a long, long way from where we were."

Improving public education and job creation are ongoing issues, he said.

And then there were the issues that were unexpected. He said his [call to ban ](https://substack.com/redirect/921cef4e-1b44-490e-9a05-a9b98db8485a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)consumable hemp-based THC products is an example.

"This is a dangerous drug that we must ban. You can't regulate it," he said. "If you regulate something, you legalize it...we do not want Texas to be California or Colorado. We do not want to be the marijuana capital of the country."

"We're always having to look at the cultural issues of the day that some people fight back on," Patrick added. Republicans don't start these cultural fights. We just finished them."

"When you have 30 million people, the seventh or eighth largest economy in the world, and you only have 31 people to do it, the work is never done," Patrick said." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/5d80bf2a-b907-4f85-baf3-568c3e76a2a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

**" Feds demand Ken Paxton's Senate campaign explain more than 100 potentially illegal contributions," **San Antonio Current's **Sanford Nowlin** \-- "Federal regulators are asking Texas Attorney General**Ken Paxton's** U.S. Senate Campaign to explain or return roughly $658,000 in political contributions that appear to violate federal law.

The [Aug. 17 letter](https://substack.com/redirect/415be3fd-6d8e-4976-afab-bba2714112e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the Federal Election Commission gives the Ken Paxton for Senate campaign a little more than a month to justify five pages worth of second-quarter contributions that -- as recorded in its paperwork -- exceed federal limits on how much individuals donors can contribute.

Under federal law, individuals are barred from making contributions to a candidate that exceed $3,500 per election. Yet the FEC letter identifies more than 100 contributions to the Republican's senate campaign that exceed the limit -- many by thousands of dollars. Among the contributions called into question is one from former state lawmaker, Texas Tech chancellor and AT&T executive **John Montford** , who donated $7,000 to Paxton's Senate campaign during the quarter.

The FEC letter also asks the Paxton campaign and its treasurer, **John Plishka** , to offer more details about contributions from five businesses listed in its second-quarter filings as limited liability companies, or LLCs. Under federal law, LLCs may only donate to campaigns if the IRS considers them to be organized as partnerships rather than corporations.

The Current reached out to the Paxton campaign for comment early Monday afternoon but received no response by press time.

The FEC letter gives Plishka until Sept. 22 to either explain the contributions, return them or refile the campaign's paperwork so they're correctly accounted for.

"Failure to adequately respond by the response date noted above could result in an audit or enforcement action," the letter states.

"Although the Commission may take further legal action concerning the acceptance of prohibited contributions, your prompt action to refund the prohibited amount will be taken into consideration," the FEC also warns.

FEC letters asking campaigns to explain their filings aren't uncommon, but this one includes an unusually long list of questionable contributions, campaign finance experts note. What's more, it comes as questions about corruption and Paxton's political and business ethics swirl around the campaign.

During his tenure as Attorney General, Paxton was impeached by the Texas House over allegations of bribery and abuse of office, although he was [later acquitted](https://substack.com/redirect/63fbe549-a5c0-444e-bcf3-e18eae81e51f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by the state Senate. Further, Paxton [agreed in March](https://substack.com/redirect/d09206b0-3ed9-4651-83c0-aedf2e195e17?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to settle a years' long securities-fraud case, and in April, a Travis County district judge [awarded $6.6 million](https://substack.com/redirect/24d4fd5f-0ea0-4609-946e-efe670539a46?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to four of the AG's former aides, who said he improperly fired them after they reported his conduct to the FBI.

Paxton's Republican primary rival, U.S. Sen. **John Cornyn** , has so far shown a willingness to bring up corruption concerns. While the FEC letter may not prove as powerful a weapon as an impeachment or court judgment, political experts said it could figure into a larger campaign.

"Cornyn will try to make as much hay as possible with this," University of Texas at San Antonio political scientist **Jon Taylor** said of the letter.

Southern Methodist University political scientist **Cal Jillson** said red flags in Paxton's second-quarter filing likely stem from the AG being accustomed to Texas campaign finance rules, which include no upper limits on individual contributions. The FEC letter could prove a potential liability if Cornyn or another Paxton rival can figure out how to effectively weaponize it, the professor added.

"In politics, it's rarely what you did but what your opponent makes of it," Jillson said." [SA Current](https://substack.com/redirect/4ec9baed-68a1-48f4-b4dd-41c1984aee72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **BUSINESS NEWS**

**" Next Starship launch from South Texas as soon as Aug. 24, SpaceX says," **San Antonio Express-News' **Brandon Lingle** \-- "SpaceX is preparing for the next flight of its Starship mega-rocket from [South Texas](https://substack.com/redirect/00b2e939-b374-44a8-a12f-3ad70af77414?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as soon as Aug. 24, a date that would put it nearly three months after its last mission failed to meet its goals.

The private space company made the announcement Friday after receiving launch approval from the Federal Aviation Administration.

Starship was grounded after its last test flight on May 27. While the launch and separation of that flight's upper and lower stages appeared to be flawless, SpaceX lost contact with the upper-stage Starship when it burst apart about 45 minutes into the suborbital flight.

The company led an FAA-mandated probe into the mishap with help from NASA, the National Transportation Safety Board and Space Force.

In a [summary of the investigation](https://substack.com/redirect/08891f80-be87-49b2-b53a-ece0c618e78e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) issued Friday, the company said problems with the craft's fuel system led to control issues and methane leaks. As a result, Starship was out of control when it reentered the atmosphere and broke up over the Indian Ocean.

A failure on the main fuel tank pressurization system diffuser likely caused the loss, it said, and that part has since been redesigned.

Also during that flight, the lower-stage booster exploded while descending toward the Gulf of Mexico as the company tried to gather data on the "limits of the booster's performance."

It was the third-straight failure for the rocket system CEO **Elon Musk** says will colonize Mars. All three were blamed on issues with the ship's fuel systems.

The next launch will be the fourth this year and 10th overall for Starship, which is launched from the private company's Starbase facility near Boca Chica Beach.

SpaceX also said Friday that "undetectable or under screened damage" to a pressure tank led to a June 18 testing explosion that destroyed the Starship it intended to fly on the upcoming mission. The spectacular blast damaged the test site and lobbed debris across the Rio Grande into Mexico." [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/7752e6c5-2343-468c-bb8e-ad7041d271be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

**" Irving-based Nexstar to buy rival Tegna for $3.54 billion in big local-TV deal," **via Reuters**** \-- "[Irving-based Nexstar Media](https://substack.com/redirect/a2b26451-4e91-4d03-b313-5e95c6f78f98?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) has agreed to buy smaller rival Tegna for $3.54 billion, creating a local-TV powerhouse that seeks to compete better with big tech and national media for advertising dollars.

Acquiring [Tegna](https://substack.com/redirect/fd637a00-3fc2-44bd-9970-3a1f7a137250?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) will expand Nexstar's presence in nine of the top 10 U.S. markets, covering 80% of TV households across key geographies, including Dallas, Atlanta, Phoenix and Seattle.

That includes WFAA, the ABC affiliate for the Dallas-Fort Worth market.

That could give Nexstar, the largest U.S. regional TV station operator, more leverage with advertisers and pay-TV distributors at a time when local media is grappling with falling revenue and subscriber loss because of the popularity of streaming services.

Shares of Nexstar rose 7% in early trading, while those of Tegna gained nearly 5% to trade near the offer price of $22 per share. The offer values Tegna at $6.2 billion, including debt, and marks a 44% premium to the stock's closing price on Aug. 8, before reports of a possible deal emerged.

The transaction is the latest in a wave of media M&A as firms once reliant on steady cable TV profits prepare for a streaming future. Earlier this year, [Skydance](https://substack.com/redirect/4ca3dfb2-b9e4-43fa-9f69-cc968ca0f29a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) merged with Paramount, while Comcast and Warner Bros Discovery have announced plans to split their businesses.

Tegna's history has deep Dallas roots. The parent company of _The Dallas Morning News,_ formerly named Belo Corp., bought Dallas' KBTV in 1950 and later renamed it WFAA to match its radio station in the market. Belo continued to acquire stations across the country in cities such as Houston, Seattle, Tulsa and Chattanooga before selling the stations to Gannett Co. in 2013 for $1.5 billion, a combination of TV assets that would later become Tegna.

Nexstar and Tegna are betting on looser antitrust policies under President Donald Trump to push their deal in a sector long wary of local news consolidation.

The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) said in June it was seeking to refresh a rule that caps station ownership at a combined reach of 39% of U.S. television households. An appeals court also recently struck down the FCC's "Top Four" rule, which barred ownership of two top-rated stations in the same market.

"The initiatives being pursued by the Trump administration offer local broadcasters the opportunity to expand reach, level the playing field, and compete more effectively with the Big Tech and legacy Big Media companies," Nexstar CEO **Perry Sook** [said](https://substack.com/redirect/92c306db-e46e-4e4f-9c7c-82929e6ba332?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

Nexstar owns or partners with more than 200 stations and operates brands such as The CW and NewsNation, while Tegna runs 64 stations and networks, including True Crime Network and Quest." [Reuters](https://substack.com/redirect/6d565389-8742-49c1-a5c2-1d9111fc1735?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
