TOP NEWS " Texas House set to vote on redistricting maps Wednesday," Dallas Morning News'Philip Jankowski \-- "The Texas House is set to vote on congressional redistricting Wednesday, setting up a political fight over a plan that could be critical for Republicans to hold on to a majority in the 2026 midterm elections. The House is rushing to approve the plan after enough Democrats returned to the Capitol to restore a constitutionally required quorum. Work had been halted since Aug. 4 in the chamber after more than 50 House Democrats left the state to block a vote on the bill. The redistricting plan, which advanced out of a committee late Tuesday, reshapes urban districts in North Texas, Houston, San Antonio and Austin. Several districts that have heavily favored Democrats would be transformed into districts expected to vote for Republicans by wide margins. In North Texas, the plan aimed at Democratic U.S. House Reps.Marc Veasey a ndJulie Johnson. Veasey was moved out of his district while Johnson's district was stretched to include a wide swath of northeast Texas, turning it from a safe Democratic district to one that would have voted for President Donald Trump by 41 percentage points. House lawmakers redrawing the maps are led by Rep. Todd Hunter , R-Corpus Christi. Hunter's latest iteration of the map made public Monday makes further alterations to the Houston area. There, Republicans are set to gain one seat in Congress by transforming the district represented by Democrat Rep. Al Green from an urban southern Houston district that favored former Vice President Kamala Harris in the 2024 election by 44 percentage points to a district that combines shards of eastern Houston with a swath of rural east Texas that stretches 60 miles to the north and east. The new district would have voted for Trump over Harris by nearly 20 percentage points. Compared to a previous draft plan, it pushes what was the most drastic change in the partisan makeup of a district even further to the right. Trump has called for Texas to flip five seats to the GOP. If Democrats gain control of the U.S. House, it would likely kneecap his ability to enact his agenda in the final two years of his term. Congressional Democrats would also gain the ability to launch investigations into his administration or possibly impeach Trump again. On Tuesday, Trump praised the redistricting effort on social media, thanking Gov. Greg Abbott , Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick and House Speaker Dustin Burrows , R-Lubbock. "It is wonderful to see Republicans everywhere standing up to Save our Country. It is one of the most popular initiatives I have ever supported. Republicans love watching us fight for a Great Cause. Please pass this Map, ASAP," Trump said." ($) FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. Free subscribers will need to become paid subscribers to receive full story summaries. TOP NEWS " Measles outbreak is over, Texas health officials say" " Do state talks signal new hope for S.A. to Austin commuter rail?" ($) " Houston to get rare August cold front that could trigger flooding rainfall" ($) " Texas judge scraps decision listing lesser prairie chicken as threatened" ($) #TXLEGE " Texas Democrat spends night in Legislature protesting police shadowing in redistricting battle" " Texas Senate fast tracks THC ban, flood prep and bathroom restrictions for transgender people" STATE GOVERNMENT " Appeals court overturns order that stripped some protections from pregnant Texas state workers" " Texas prison agency names senior official as its next chief," AP's Alex Nguyen \-- "Bobby Lumpkin will be the Texas Department of Criminal Justice's next executive director, leading one of the largest state agencies that is also in the midst of a correctional staffing crisis. Lumpkin, who is currently the agency's chief operations officer, will assume the top role on Sept. 1, TDCJ's governing board announced Tuesday. He replaces Executive Director Bryan Collier , who is set to retire on Aug. 31 after nine years on the job. Lumpkin, who as a correctional officer, will soon head an agency that oversees over 100 prisons and around 140,000 inmates. This work is taking place amid a statewide , including high vacancy and turnover rates that could pose risks to employees and inmates, according to from the Texas Sunset Advisory Commission. The state Legislature has recently for TDCJ that would go toward pay raises, targeted salary adjustments and other initiatives to address staffing. He will also have to continue addressing the lack of air conditioning in prisons, which a federal judge has ruled earlier this year as and pushed for a trial. TDCJ is installing cool beds, but the majority of Texas prisons are still not fully air conditioned. The staffing shortage also to the challenge. A trial over the issue is currently scheduled to take place next year. At the same time, Lumpkin will take the reins of an agency that is undergoing a , as outlined in its "2030 Vision" plan. Collier his intention to retire in June. He first joined the agency as a clerk in 1985 and rose through its ranks over four decades, before being named its leader in 2016. On Tuesday, the TDCJ's governing board also named in Huntsville after him. As of July, Collier's as executive director was nearly $300,000." LOCAL GOVERNMENT " No employee benefit cuts, but Harris County targets departments for reductions amid $48M budget deficit," Houston Chronicle's John Lomax V \-- "Harris County's deficit remains at roughly $48 million The county's $2.7 billion budget will likely need to be balanced by cuts to some of the county's major departments, including Harris County Public Health, Engineering and Housing and Community Development, according to an internal email shared with the Houston Chronicle. It's the latest move in a game of financial Jenga that's driven a rift between the court's four Democrats. JudgeLina Hidalgo and Commissioner Rodney Ellis have voiced concerns that the proposed budget would slash crucial services, while Commissioners Adrian Garcia and Lesley Briones advocated cutting vacant positions and implementing common-sense efficiencies that they allege would have a negligible impact on county-run programs. "We all know we have a deficit, and what we have done is lean in to being solutions-oriented -- finding ways we could do more with less, finding those savings," Briones said during a news conference held Thursday morning. "Some would like you to believe that the sky has fallen, that there will be devastating cuts in this dystopian universe, false." While Garcia and Briones lobbed vague accusations of unnamed actors seeking to spread "false narratives," Hidalgo told reporters at a separate news conference just 30 minutes later that Briones and Garcia -- along with Republican CommissionerTom Ramse y -- were attempting to steer the narrative in their favor. Hidalgo accused the pair of obfuscating the impact of a previous vote to provide sweeping raises to county law enforcement officers. The raises were intended to prevent an exodus of deputies to the Houston Police Department, which triggered a domino effect following the city's approval of a across five years. "Our hands are tied because my colleagues decided not to go to the voters on this $108 million increase for law enforcement that we couldn't afford," Hidalgo said. "Not only are elected leaders putting your county in the hole, but they are refusing to ask you whether you would like to see cuts. We are forcing you to have cuts." Although the raises were initially projected to cost the county roughly $140 million, that estimate proved to be an additional $30 million more than the actual cost." ($) " Mayor Jones ramps up public pressure to delay Spurs arena deal," San Antonio Express-News' Devyani Chhetri \-- "Mayor Gina Ortiz Jones isn't here to make friends. She tried to upstage Councilman Marc Whyte -- one of her main foils on City Council -- at his Northeast Side district's budget town hall on Monday and turn his residents against him over his support for approving a deal with the Spurs over a downtown arena. Jones is calling on the City Council on Thursday that would halt a vote on a proposed term sheet with Spurs Sports & Entertainment. Before the council weighs in, she wants an independent economic impact report on the arena and for each council member to hold two town halls in their district to gather constituents' opinions on the study and the proposed deal. "The Spurs want (the arena) to cost $1.3 billion. You know what you would be on the hook for? $489 million," Jones told residents who showed up to the Northeast Senior Center to talk about their spending priorities for the next city budget. "I think you deserve an opportunity to review the data, review the draft term sheet before your councilman votes on it," she continued. "And if you agree with me, please encourage him to support my resolution on Thursday." Whyte is one of five council members who want the council to -- instead of going along with Jones' resolution -- approve a dueling resolution Thursday that would authorize City Manager Erik Walsh to execute . The District 10 councilman believes the terms must be finalized before Bexar County voters to decide whether to increase the county hotel occupancy tax to raise $311 million to help build an up to 18,500-seat NBA at Hemisfair. The Spurs would kick in $500 million and pay for any cost overruns, according to the proposed terms, which were made public Monday. The city's $489M portion would be financed with bond debt that would be repaid by ground and facility leases with SS&E, property tax revenue from surrounding development guaranteed by the franchise and state hotel tax revenue. The city's contribution does not include the roughly $60 million the city expects to pay to acquire the former Institute of Texan Cultures site. The arena would be the centerpiece of a larger sports and entertainment district proposed by city staff known as Project Marvel. Whyte was visibly frustrated by Jones' remarks. He told attendees that "the dishonesty unfortunately continues" because the mayor's chief of staff told him Jones would exclusively speak about the budget. "We had an agreement literally five minutes ago that we would allow the mayor to come up and speak if we kept it to the budget and not on Project Marvel -- and yet we just saw what took place," Whyte said. That prompted Jones to return to the front of the room. She wagged her finger "no," made faces and laughed while he spoke, and at times attempted to talk over him. It was par for the course in how the two have interacted since she took office June 18. She has publicly admonished him for standing up before she has adjourned a meeting. And they frequently interrupt one another at meetings, such as when she grilled him earlier this month over how the deal's numbers would add up. The District 10 budget town hall coincided with one in District 3. Jones told the San Antonio Express-News that she plans to attend over the next two weeks. Whyte told the newspaper that the mayor's behavior was a continuation of her "PR campaign against moving forward with the term sheet." Hours earlier, Jones gathered community organizers who oppose spending public dollars on Project Marvel to as she made her pitch for why council members should pass her resolution -- rather than the one OKing the terms of the deal. She characterized council members who want to rush the deal as not "serious about representing their residents." "I'm very shocked that folks would want to move forward on this absent from hearing from their constituents," Jones said at her press conference. Jones dodged reporters' questions on whether she has the votes to pass her resolution. She needs the support of at least five council members. Asked how the votes on the competing resolutions would play out Thursday, she simply said, "We'll see." However, beyond ramping up public pressure on the council, it appears that she has done little privately to persuade those in favor of approving the terms to come to her side. "I don't know who she has talked to," Whyte told the Express-News. "Her style thus far has simply been to issue memos that show up in our inbox." The strained relationship between Jones and some of the council was on display at his budget town hall. Whyte invited Councilwomen Teri Castillo and Marina Alderete Gavito , who represent the city's West and near Northwest Sides, respectively, to the event. The trio teamed up last month to force Jones to hold a meeting to debate changes she made to how council members can propose new policies, programs and municipal laws -- changes that after it became clear that she lacked the support to have them followed. It was a short-lived win for the three. The next day, Jones passed over each of them when she , a coveted position that former mayors have given to more-senior council members. Alderete Gavito and Castillo sang Whyte's praises before his residents, even though the progressive Castillo is often at odds with the conservative councilman. They also spoke about the need to work together at City Hall. "Being on council is a team sport, and together we're getting things done for y'all," Alderete Gavito said. "It's about collaborating, working with each other to ensure we're well aware of what those needs are throughout the city of San Antonio," Castillo said." ($) 2026 " Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick launches campaign for reelection," San Antonio Current's Sanford Nowlin \-- "Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick on Tuesday launched his campaign for reelection, vowing to continue to push conservative, Christian-based legislation that protects children and taxpayers. "I'm not stepping back at any point in time," Patrick, 75, said during a news conference at The Austin Club before joining supporters for a campaign kickoff reception. "I'm all in. I've always been all in." First elected in 2014, Patrick is seeking his fourth term as lieutenant governor. As the presiding officer of the Senate, he touches every piece of major legislation, and most analysts contend he's been the most powerful legislative force in Texas. Already by President Donald Trump , Patrick on Tuesday touted the conservative legislation approved during his tenure, including property tax relief, strict anti-abortion laws, education reform and laws that protect children. Standing with him were at least seven senators up for reelection, as well as three candidates who he's endorsed. "I don't get the credit," Patrick said of his accomplishments. "I just get the privilege to lead them." Patrick also addressed rumors he wouldn't seek reelection, even as he's previously insisted he's running next year for another four-year term. "I didn't raise $5 million in five days in June if I wasn't running," he said. "I didn't accumulate, which will be $40 to $45 million in our bank account not to run." Patrick said news about his "campaign announcement" created more speculation. He described a text message he received from a friend who runs a Bible mission in Hanoi, Vietnam. "I hear you're not running," the message said. "I hear that you're going to be the senior pastor of Second Baptist Church." Patrick said there have been all kinds of rumors about his future. "I hear that maybe there was a health issue. I don't have any health issues, folks. I'm in great health," Patrick said. "Tell all those would-be opponents out there Dan's in. He's in it to win it as always," Patrick said. "We're going to be victorious in the primary. We're going to be victorious in the general, and we're just going to make Texas even more prosperous and safer and better in the future." Patrick does not have a major primary challenger. Democrat Vikki Goodwi n, D-Dallas, is planning to challenge him in the general election. Patrick said he would use another term to continue to bring Texans property tax relief. "We need to continue to reduce property taxes," Patrick said. "We've come a long, long way from where we were." Improving public education and job creation are ongoing issues, he said. And then there were the issues that were unexpected. He said his hemp-based THC products is an example. "This is a dangerous drug that we must ban. You can't regulate it," he said. "If you regulate something, you legalize it...we do not want Texas to be California or Colorado. We do not want to be the marijuana capital of the country." "We're always having to look at the cultural issues of the day that some people fight back on," Patrick added. Republicans don't start these cultural fights. We just finished them." "When you have 30 million people, the seventh or eighth largest economy in the world, and you only have 31 people to do it, the work is never done," Patrick said." ($) " Feds demand Ken Paxton's Senate campaign explain more than 100 potentially illegal contributions," San Antonio Current's Sanford Nowlin \-- "Federal regulators are asking Texas Attorney GeneralKen Paxton's U.S. Senate Campaign to explain or return roughly $658,000 in political contributions that appear to violate federal law. The from the Federal Election Commission gives the Ken Paxton for Senate campaign a little more than a month to justify five pages worth of second-quarter contributions that -- as recorded in its paperwork -- exceed federal limits on how much individuals donors can contribute. Under federal law, individuals are barred from making contributions to a candidate that exceed $3,500 per election. Yet the FEC letter identifies more than 100 contributions to the Republican's senate campaign that exceed the limit -- many by thousands of dollars. Among the contributions called into question is one from former state lawmaker, Texas Tech chancellor and AT&T executive John Montford , who donated $7,000 to Paxton's Senate campaign during the quarter. The FEC letter also asks the Paxton campaign and its treasurer, John Plishka , to offer more details about contributions from five businesses listed in its second-quarter filings as limited liability companies, or LLCs. Under federal law, LLCs may only donate to campaigns if the IRS considers them to be organized as partnerships rather than corporations. The Current reached out to the Paxton campaign for comment early Monday afternoon but received no response by press time. The FEC letter gives Plishka until Sept. 22 to either explain the contributions, return them or refile the campaign's paperwork so they're correctly accounted for. "Failure to adequately respond by the response date noted above could result in an audit or enforcement action," the letter states. "Although the Commission may take further legal action concerning the acceptance of prohibited contributions, your prompt action to refund the prohibited amount will be taken into consideration," the FEC also warns. FEC letters asking campaigns to explain their filings aren't uncommon, but this one includes an unusually long list of questionable contributions, campaign finance experts note. What's more, it comes as questions about corruption and Paxton's political and business ethics swirl around the campaign. During his tenure as Attorney General, Paxton was impeached by the Texas House over allegations of bribery and abuse of office, although he was by the state Senate. Further, Paxton to settle a years' long securities-fraud case, and in April, a Travis County district judge to four of the AG's former aides, who said he improperly fired them after they reported his conduct to the FBI. Paxton's Republican primary rival, U.S. Sen. John Cornyn , has so far shown a willingness to bring up corruption concerns. While the FEC letter may not prove as powerful a weapon as an impeachment or court judgment, political experts said it could figure into a larger campaign. "Cornyn will try to make as much hay as possible with this," University of Texas at San Antonio political scientist Jon Taylor said of the letter. Southern Methodist University political scientist Cal Jillson said red flags in Paxton's second-quarter filing likely stem from the AG being accustomed to Texas campaign finance rules, which include no upper limits on individual contributions. The FEC letter could prove a potential liability if Cornyn or another Paxton rival can figure out how to effectively weaponize it, the professor added. "In politics, it's rarely what you did but what your opponent makes of it," Jillson said." BUSINESS NEWS " Next Starship launch from South Texas as soon as Aug. 24, SpaceX says," San Antonio Express-News' Brandon Lingle \-- "SpaceX is preparing for the next flight of its Starship mega-rocket from as soon as Aug. 24, a date that would put it nearly three months after its last mission failed to meet its goals. The private space company made the announcement Friday after receiving launch approval from the Federal Aviation Administration. Starship was grounded after its last test flight on May 27. While the launch and separation of that flight's upper and lower stages appeared to be flawless, SpaceX lost contact with the upper-stage Starship when it burst apart about 45 minutes into the suborbital flight. The company led an FAA-mandated probe into the mishap with help from NASA, the National Transportation Safety Board and Space Force. In a issued Friday, the company said problems with the craft's fuel system led to control issues and methane leaks. As a result, Starship was out of control when it reentered the atmosphere and broke up over the Indian Ocean. A failure on the main fuel tank pressurization system diffuser likely caused the loss, it said, and that part has since been redesigned. Also during that flight, the lower-stage booster exploded while descending toward the Gulf of Mexico as the company tried to gather data on the "limits of the booster's performance." It was the third-straight failure for the rocket system CEO Elon Musk says will colonize Mars. All three were blamed on issues with the ship's fuel systems. The next launch will be the fourth this year and 10th overall for Starship, which is launched from the private company's Starbase facility near Boca Chica Beach. SpaceX also said Friday that "undetectable or under screened damage" to a pressure tank led to a June 18 testing explosion that destroyed the Starship it intended to fly on the upcoming mission. The spectacular blast damaged the test site and lobbed debris across the Rio Grande into Mexico." ($) " Irving-based Nexstar to buy rival Tegna for $3.54 billion in big local-TV deal," via Reuters \-- " has agreed to buy smaller rival Tegna for $3.54 billion, creating a local-TV powerhouse that seeks to compete better with big tech and national media for advertising dollars. Acquiring will expand Nexstar's presence in nine of the top 10 U.S. markets, covering 80% of TV households across key geographies, including Dallas, Atlanta, Phoenix and Seattle. That includes WFAA, the ABC affiliate for the Dallas-Fort Worth market. That could give Nexstar, the largest U.S. regional TV station operator, more leverage with advertisers and pay-TV distributors at a time when local media is grappling with falling revenue and subscriber loss because of the popularity of streaming services. Shares of Nexstar rose 7% in early trading, while those of Tegna gained nearly 5% to trade near the offer price of $22 per share. The offer values Tegna at $6.2 billion, including debt, and marks a 44% premium to the stock's closing price on Aug. 8, before reports of a possible deal emerged. The transaction is the latest in a wave of media M&A as firms once reliant on steady cable TV profits prepare for a streaming future. Earlier this year, merged with Paramount, while Comcast and Warner Bros Discovery have announced plans to split their businesses. Tegna's history has deep Dallas roots. The parent company of The Dallas Morning News, formerly named Belo Corp., bought Dallas' KBTV in 1950 and later renamed it WFAA to match its radio station in the market. Belo continued to acquire stations across the country in cities such as Houston, Seattle, Tulsa and Chattanooga before selling the stations to Gannett Co. in 2013 for $1.5 billion, a combination of TV assets that would later become Tegna. Nexstar and Tegna are betting on looser antitrust policies under President Donald Trump to push their deal in a sector long wary of local news consolidation. The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) said in June it was seeking to refresh a rule that caps station ownership at a combined reach of 39% of U.S. television households. An appeals court also recently struck down the FCC's "Top Four" rule, which barred ownership of two top-rated stations in the same market. "The initiatives being pursued by the Trump administration offer local broadcasters the opportunity to expand reach, level the playing field, and compete more effectively with the Big Tech and legacy Big Media companies," Nexstar CEO Perry Sook . Nexstar owns or partners with more than 200 stations and operates brands such as The CW and NewsNation, while Tegna runs 64 stations and networks, including True Crime Network and Quest." * **TOP NEWS**

**" Texas House set to vote on redistricting maps Wednesday," **Dallas Morning News'**Philip Jankowski** \-- "The Texas House is set to vote on congressional redistricting Wednesday, setting up a political fight over a plan that could be critical for Republicans to hold on to a majority in the 2026 midterm elections.

The House is rushing to approve the plan after enough Democrats returned to the Capitol to restore a constitutionally required quorum. Work had been halted since Aug. 4 in the chamber after more than 50 House Democrats left the state to block a vote on the bill.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/c433b65d-0a17-4a72-94cf-7ad4cefa5c59?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The redistricting plan, which advanced out of a committee late Tuesday, reshapes urban districts in North Texas, Houston, San Antonio and Austin. Several districts that have heavily favored Democrats would be transformed into districts expected to vote for Republicans by wide margins.

In North Texas, the plan aimed at Democratic U.S. House Reps.**Marc Veasey a** nd**Julie Johnson**. Veasey was moved out of his district while Johnson's district was stretched to include a wide swath of northeast Texas, turning it from a safe Democratic district to one that would have voted for President **Donald Trump** by 41 percentage points.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/c433b65d-0a17-4a72-94cf-7ad4cefa5c59?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
House lawmakers redrawing the maps are led by Rep. **Todd Hunter** , R-Corpus Christi. Hunter's latest iteration of the map made public Monday makes further alterations to the Houston area.

There, Republicans are set to gain one seat in Congress by transforming the district represented by Democrat Rep**. Al Green** from an urban southern Houston district that favored former Vice President **Kamala Harris** in the 2024 election by 44 percentage points to a district that combines shards of eastern Houston with a swath of rural east Texas that stretches 60 miles to the north and east.

The new district would have voted for Trump over Harris by nearly 20 percentage points. Compared to a previous draft plan, it pushes what was the most drastic change in the partisan makeup of a district even further to the right.

Trump has called for Texas to flip five seats to the GOP. If Democrats gain control of the U.S. House, it would likely kneecap his ability to enact his agenda in the final two years of his term. Congressional Democrats would also gain the ability to launch investigations into his administration or possibly impeach Trump again.

On Tuesday, Trump praised the redistricting effort on social media, thanking Gov. **Greg Abbott** , Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** and House Speaker **Dustin Burrows** , R-Lubbock.

"It is wonderful to see Republicans everywhere standing up to Save our Country. It is one of the most popular initiatives I have ever supported. Republicans love watching us fight for a Great Cause. Please pass this Map, ASAP," Trump said." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/86e9acc5-c9ec-4b41-a146-223d016f04f8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

TOP NEWS " Texas House set to vote on redistricting maps Wednesday," Dallas Morning News'Philip Jankowski \-- "The Texas House is set to vote on congressional redistricting Wednesday, setting up a political fight over a plan that could be critical for Republicans to hold on to a majority in the 2026 midterm elections. The House is rushing to approve the plan after enough Democrats returned to the Capitol to restore a constitutionally required quorum. Work had been halted since Aug. 4 in the chamber after more than 50 House Democrats left the state to block a vote on the bill. The redistricting plan, which advanced out of a committee late Tuesday, reshapes urban districts in North Texas, Houston, San Antonio and Austin. Several districts that have heavily favored Democrats would be transformed into districts expected to vote for Republicans by wide margins. In North Texas, the plan aimed at Democratic U.S. House Reps.Marc Veasey a ndJulie Johnson. Veasey was moved out of his district while Johnson's district was stretched to include a wide swath of northeast Texas, turning it from a safe Democratic district to one that would have voted for President Donald Trump by 41 percentage points. House lawmakers redrawing the maps are led by Rep. Todd Hunter , R-Corpus Christi. Hunter's latest iteration of the map made public Monday makes further alterations to the Houston area. There, Republicans are set to gain one seat in Congress by transforming the district represented by Democrat Rep. Al Green from an urban southern Houston district that favored former Vice President Kamala Harris in the 2024 election by 44 percentage points to a district that combines shards of eastern Houston with a swath of rural east Texas that stretches 60 miles to the north and east. The new district would have voted for Trump over Harris by nearly 20 percentage points. Compared to a previous draft plan, it pushes what was the most drastic change in the partisan makeup of a district even further to the right. Trump has called for Texas to flip five seats to the GOP. If Democrats gain control of the U.S. House, it would likely kneecap his ability to enact his agenda in the final two years of his term. Congressional Democrats would also gain the ability to launch investigations into his administration or possibly impeach Trump again. On Tuesday, Trump praised the redistricting effort on social media, thanking Gov. Greg Abbott , Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick and House Speaker Dustin Burrows , R-Lubbock. "It is wonderful to see Republicans everywhere standing up to Save our Country. It is one of the most popular initiatives I have ever supported. Republicans love watching us fight for a Great Cause. Please pass this Map, ASAP," Trump said." ($) FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. Free subscribers will need to become paid subscribers to receive full story summaries. **“Texas House set to vote on redistricting maps Wednesday,”** Dallas Morning News'**Philip Jankowski** \-- "The Texas House is set to vote on congressional redistricting Wednesday, setting up a political fight over a plan that could be critical for Republicans to hold on to a majority in the 2026 midterm elections.

The House is rushing to approve the plan after enough Democrats returned to the Capitol to restore a constitutionally required quorum. Work had been halted since Aug. 4 in the chamber after more than 50 House Democrats left the state to block a vote on the bill.

The redistricting plan, which advanced out of a committee late Tuesday, reshapes urban districts in North Texas, Houston, San Antonio and Austin. Several districts that have heavily favored Democrats would be transformed into districts expected to vote for Republicans by wide margins.

In North Texas, the plan aimed at Democratic U.S. House Reps.**Marc Veasey a** nd**Julie Johnson**. Veasey was moved out of his district while Johnson’s district was stretched to include a wide swath of northeast Texas, turning it from a safe Democratic district to one that would have voted for President **Donald Trump** by 41 percentage points.

House lawmakers redrawing the maps are led by Rep. **Todd Hunter** , R-Corpus Christi. Hunter’s latest iteration of the map made public Monday makes further alterations to the Houston area.

There, Republicans are set to gain one seat in Congress by transforming the district represented by Democrat Rep**. Al Green** from an urban southern Houston district that favored former Vice President **Kamala Harris** in the 2024 election by 44 percentage points to a district that combines shards of eastern Houston with a swath of rural east Texas that stretches 60 miles to the north and east.

The new district would have voted for Trump over Harris by nearly 20 percentage points. Compared to a previous draft plan, it pushes what was the most drastic change in the partisan makeup of a district even further to the right.

Trump has called for Texas to flip five seats to the GOP. If Democrats gain control of the U.S. House, it would likely kneecap his ability to enact his agenda in the final two years of his term. Congressional Democrats would also gain the ability to launch investigations into his administration or possibly impeach Trump again.

On Tuesday, Trump praised the redistricting effort on social media, thanking Gov. **Greg Abbott** , Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** and House Speaker **Dustin Burrows** , R-Lubbock.

“It is wonderful to see Republicans everywhere standing up to Save our Country. It is one of the most popular initiatives I have ever supported. Republicans love watching us fight for a Great Cause. Please pass this Map, ASAP,” Trump said." Sign up now and invite your friends and family to be part of the informed. | TOP NEWS “Texas House set to vote on redistricting maps Wednesday,” Dallas Morning News'Philip Jankowski \-- "The Texas House is set to vote on congressional redistricting Wednesday, setting up a political fight over a plan that could be critical for Republicans to hold on to a majority in the 2026 midterm elections. The House is rushing to approve the plan after enough Democrats returned to the Capitol to restore a constitutionally required quorum. Work had been halted since Aug. 4 in the chamber after more than 50 House Democrats left the state to block a vote on the bill. » The redistricting plan, which advanced out of a committee late Tuesday, reshapes urban districts in North Texas, Houston, San Antonio and Austin. Several districts that have heavily favored Democrats would be transformed into districts expected to vote for Republicans by wide margins. In North Texas, the plan aimed at Democratic U.S. House Reps.Marc Veasey a ndJulie Johnson. Veasey was moved out of his district while Johnson’s district was stretched to include a wide swath of northeast Texas, turning it from a safe Democratic district to one that would have voted for President Donald Trump by 41 percentage points. » House lawmakers redrawing the maps are led by Rep. Todd Hunter , R-Corpus Christi. Hunter’s latest iteration of the map made public Monday makes further alterations to the Houston area. There, Republicans are set to gain one seat in Congress by transforming the district represented by Democrat Rep. Al Green from an urban southern Houston district that favored former Vice President Kamala Harris in the 2024 election by 44 percentage points to a district that combines shards of eastern Houston with a swath of rural east Texas that stretches 60 miles to the north and east. The new district would have voted for Trump over Harris by nearly 20 percentage points. Compared to a previous draft plan, it pushes what was the most drastic change in the partisan makeup of a district even further to the right. Trump has called for Texas to flip five seats to the GOP. If Democrats gain control of the U.S. House, it would likely kneecap his ability to enact his agenda in the final two years of his term. Congressional Democrats would also gain the ability to launch investigations into his administration or possibly impeach Trump again. On Tuesday, Trump praised the redistricting effort on social media, thanking Gov. Greg Abbott , Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick and House Speaker Dustin Burrows , R-Lubbock. “It is wonderful to see Republicans everywhere standing up to Save our Country. It is one of the most popular initiatives I have ever supported. Republicans love watching us fight for a Great Cause. Please pass this Map, ASAP,” Trump said." name: Rob Bonta
subject: an update on our SNAP lawsuit
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642]
body:
[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/4?refcode=em250819&t=1001&akid=1919%2E1088799%2EDp7RLA ]Rob Bonta

I have an update on our
lawsuit against the Trump Administration’s demand that states turn over
the private and sensitive personal information of millions of families who
rely on food assistance.

Yesterday, I asked the court to block Trump’s USDA from attempting to
bully states into sharing the private data of families who rely on SNAP —
the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program — while our litigation
continues.

When families sign up for SNAP, they provide information with the
understanding that it won’t be used for unrelated purposes. In fact,
federal and state laws guarantee that this information can’t be used for
unrelated purposes.

But the Trump administration is disregarding the law and demanding that
states turn over SNAP recipients’ social security numbers, home addresses,
and even shopping history going back five years.

And now they’re threatening to cut off $1.4 billion in funding for
California’s SNAP program if we don’t comply.

It’s illegal, plain and simple — and that’s why I’m suing. [ https://act.robbonta.com/survey/em-250819-pet/?t=1002&akid=1919%2E1088799%2EDp7RLA ]If you’re
with me, please sign my petition today.

The idea that families could be forced to choose between feeding their
children and protecting their privacy is despicable.

This was never about preventing “waste, fraud, and abuse.” Even Trump’s
own USDA admits SNAP already has one of the most rigorous oversight
systems in government. This demand is about creating fear and driving
families away from programs they need to survive.

California will not be bullied into violating the law or betraying
families who rely on this vital program.

[ https://act.robbonta.com/survey/em-250819-pet/?t=1003&akid=1919%2E1088799%2EDp7RLA ]If you agree that Trump must be held accountable for his blatant
violations of the law and the Constitution, please add your name today.

[ https://act.robbonta.com/survey/em-250819-pet/?t=1004&akid=1919%2E1088799%2EDp7RLA ]ADD YOUR NAME

Thanks for standing with me.

Rob

[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/1029?t=1005&akid=1919%2E1088799%2EDp7RLA ][IMG]

Rob Bonta
Attorney General of California

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.robbonta.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=1006&akid=1919%2E1088799%2EDp7RLA
Update Your Information: https://act.robbonta.com/signup/update-your-information/
Unsubscribe: https://act.robbonta.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1007&akid=1919%2E1088799%2EDp7RLA

c/o Deane & Company
1700 Tribute Road, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA 95815
info@robbonta.com

body:
Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District.
https://ericswalwell.com [https://ericswalwell.com]
Hey there -

You know what Republicans have called themselves for years? The "law and order" party?

Well, MAGA has shown us just how much that is all B.S.

Why? Because Trump just gutted police and public safety funding in California and is defunding crime-prevention policies that are proven to work.

Instead of investing in what works, Trump is ramping up funding for ICE, criminalizing the unhoused, and slashing funds for the common-sense, comprehensive programs that actually work.

Things like after-school programs, community programs, and even funding for police.

So much for law and order, huh?

We’re going to be fighting back. Help us keep up the pressure, and let’s hold MAGA accountable for their nonsensical policies. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-Aug2025-FR-August?&refcode=E14789-20250819-PublicSafety-SL-Other&amount=25]

name: Brigadier General James Emory Mace, for Nancy Mace for Governor
subject: I never thought I’d write something like this.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY NANCY MACE FOR GOVERNOR
body:
Please read what I have to say about my daughter

| | | [](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z37vfteocnr0hth8ti826v078tlmfbrhrca6rdq8_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
| | [](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zbinq6mdbtnovrupbg6nlci8eh3q2k9u2lavrqi8_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
|
---
| | Hi, This is Brigadier General James Emory Mace, U.S. Army (Retired). _**Today, I write to you not as a soldier... _but as a father._**_ I'm a **Purple Heart** veteran, and I’ve seen battle. I’ve worn the uniform. I’ve buried friends who never made it home. **And I’ve felt the sting of a Nation that sometimes forgets what our sacrifices were for.** _**[But my daughter—Congresswoman Nancy Mace—never forgot.](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zpemi3bukev39pneh2p3aj5dreqobos6hhn30eag_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)**_ Nancy was raised in the shadow of the flag. She saw the uniform. She lived with the deployments. She knew the cost. _**And instead of running from it, she ran toward it.**_
---
[](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zl888ncbnunmgpnaffoc3jqeut27efcreo1mno70_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
When Nancy was 17, she dropped out of high school. Most kids in that situation fall apart. But not Nancy.**She picked herself up, took a job at Waffle House, and fought her way back.** **By 21, she became the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s Military College. A place I knew well.**
---
[](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zui0juactl5t2vikemlqepe6jpr4tkhbu47l9ab0_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
[ **SUPPORT NANCY MACE FOR GOVERNOR** ](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z7lhr9blbe53k5os123t2k3qscmkv5jvjbojmvpg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
**Nancy then made it to Congress, where _she represented the people of South Carolina’s First District._** She’s been fighting her whole life; for herself, for her kids, for the truth, and now...for her state. **[Today, Nancy is running for Governor of South Carolina. And just like she did at The Citadel, she’s marching headfirst into a battle.](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zr48jefh1nft7lrpct5d88ukuvlbkmsgeb331lag_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)** It’s hard sitting back as a father and hearing the terrible things the media and the establishment say about her. [**_But Nancy is my daughter. And I know this: She never backs down. And she never stops fighting._**](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zrn79onhnubs1trdi9kesq99a991ikgoeeg9gfco_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
[](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zcl5lgqjc31ck8ep1lukvpsi1ipdt7itglqaun0g_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
**I want to ask you something from one parent, one patriot, to another:** [**_Will you support my daughter in the toughest battle of her life?_**](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zgv4hj2pdsjc394k9it7cvc721o9nj0amndpnmbg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs) **She’s up against a machine that wants to __erase the values of faith-based, service-minded Americans who believe in truth, family, and country.__** Nancy stood up for me. She stood up for _**EVERY**_ veteran who was forgotten. She stood up when sanctuary sheriffs released illegal alien criminals onto South Carolina streets. She stood up when they tried to let biological men compete in women’s sports. She stood up when **other Republicans stayed silent.** **And now, she’s standing up for _YOU._** But she needs your backup. **_[**_I may not wear the uniform anymore,_but I still believe in answering the call.__**](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zr1u4a1mrv62urkp3d16nmi6263iecergmg10pco_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)___** Will you answer it with me today? **[Whether it’s $5, $10, or $15, your donation means everything right now.](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zfc8n8m8ta41iqjbu3otk6nacr78iggs28f5ujh0_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)** Nancy’s not running to make friends. _**She’s running to _win._**_ **To fight. To serve.** ** ** That’s what she learned growing up in our home. That’s what she learned watching me go off to war. _**And that’s what she’ll carry with her to the Governor’s Mansion.**_ Please, on this solemn day, join me in standing with my daughter. [Yes, Nancy is a warrior.**But she is also my little girl—and she needs you, Patriot**](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z2fveeadh40no55ervv64kdcmgf03vhq80rg86ao_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
[ **DONATE TO NANCY'S CAMPAIGN** ](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zqf71ftdo3h4cucdbbo10lhv1hrfrda4a2id8bdg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
With respect, General James Emory Mace (Ret.)_**Proud Father of Nancy Mace**_
---
| | [](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zq1p38rv8p2t404ql140v9bv7lng8mom3ppeckro_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
| | [ **$25** ](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z6d7st5v68idauf33aompul4g5veiljl5r2h4ctg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
[ **$50** ](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zni4mepb6brl0n3m63gsbtugfogcul3kqo3si9c8_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
[ **$100** ](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z9vq2eq8n1fuc9vcvppneshi5fnbe2p13rui5osg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
[ **OTHER AMOUNT** ](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z9mlrs5rugb4jlerc45v52k5smqiv8d5polo0rmg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2o35c9h2bs)
---
**High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.** **Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.** At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts. **By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch.** She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground.Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina. Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks. **She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.** Nancy is fighting to:
* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast
* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency
* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free
* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate
* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think
* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. **South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.**
---
| |
# PAID FOR BY NANCY MACE FOR GOVERNOR

name: Hillsdale College via Updates For Great Americans
subject: Now Available: “Ancient Christianity”on DVD
body:
These lessons are especially important today, as Christians face increasing opposition from the culture.

This sponsored message from Updates for Great Americans is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zv20jemp5d6lo183th67ipa484r1n0fvb7fuqb0o_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2p76c9h2bs)
---
| | Dear Friend,

Learning about how early Christians worshipped and prayed, faced persecution and hardship, and settled theological disputes enriches our understanding of the faith. These lessons are especially important today, as Christians face increasing opposition from the culture.

But, beyond reading the New Testament and some of the stories of early Christian martyrs, it can be daunting to figure out how to proceed. The complicated history of imperial Rome, along with the foreign names and locations featured in early Christian history, can make understanding the most important historical points difficult.

To uncover this fascinating history, we've produced our online course, "[Ancient Christianity](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zeglbspc549g17lfgo1lte3sik4g19h9p7pg64l0_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2p76c9h2bs)," now available on DVD.

In this course, Professor Kenneth Calvert is your guide through the first four centuries of Christianity as he explains the remarkable ways the new faith transformed the Roman world.

Would you help support this course so we can make it available to millions of Americans for free? As a token of our appreciation for your support, we'll send you a copy of our beautiful DVD Box Set for any donations of $100 or more.
[](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zabtd5s783a27l2trd3jhdsfgqbc1kl9jp4807do_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2p76c9h2bs)
**You can watch the video trailer for the course and get your copy of "Ancient Christianity" on DVD using the secure link below:**

[](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zrh0osp1u219easoloc44q6rfg2hblct17oiq51g_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2p76c9h2bs)**[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/ancient-christianity-dvd-trailer](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zml3epnpog1mujv51t9gq6qo15trhbguq415t1d0_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2p76c9h2bs)**

Thank you for your support in reaching millions of Americans with the story of the courage and faith of the first Christians.

Best regards,

Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D.
Executive Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College
---
| |
---

**Nancy then made it to Congress, where _she represented the people of South Carolina’s First District._** She’s been fighting her whole life; for herself, for her kids, for the truth, and now...for her state. **[Today, Nancy is running for Governor of South Carolina. [**_But Nancy is my daughter. And I know this: She never backs down. And she never stops fighting._**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z7kgc4obedfkjo6nt51e6e02k9pih7s34tq6aksg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2p71chh2bs)

**I want to ask you something from one parent, one patriot, to another:** [**_Will you support my daughter in the toughest battle of her life?_**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zq3fvio78ban5e0s2lkbrsfvvmn7as027roq33g8_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2p71chh2bs) **She’s up against a machine that wants to __erase the values of faith-based, service-minded Americans who believe in truth, family, and country.__** Nancy stood up for me. She stood up for _**EVERY**_ veteran who was forgotten. She stood up when sanctuary sheriffs released illegal alien criminals onto South Carolina streets. She stood up when they tried to let biological men compete in women’s sports. She stood up when **other Republicans stayed silent.** **And now, she’s standing up for _YOU._** But she needs your backup. **_[**_I may not wear the uniform anymore,_but I still believe in answering the call.__**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z9drqmoo9kqquj7gfna74knenjqfsdlkk7n04l3o_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2p71chh2bs)___** Will you answer it with me today? **[Whether it’s $5, $10, or $15, your donation means everything right now.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zavg5e63t8i8l8r37udem2l6bls5iomiejrrr5bg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge2p71chh2bs)** Nancy’s not running to make friends. _**She’s running to _win._**_ **To fight. To serve.** ** ** That’s what she learned growing up in our home. **High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.** **Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.** At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts. **By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch.** She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground.Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina. Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks. **She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.** Nancy is fighting to:
* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast
* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency
* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free
* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate
* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think
* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. **South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.**

name: Hillsdale College via First Look News
subject: Now Available: “Ancient Christianity”on DVD
body:
These lessons are especially important today, as Christians face increasing opposition from the culture.

This sponsored message from First Look is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zcg2fik79gmcvncttmc4lciq4ei9lo9s8oagj0bo_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdgeao72c9h2bs)
---
| | Dear Friend,

Learning about how early Christians worshipped and prayed, faced persecution and hardship, and settled theological disputes enriches our understanding of the faith. These lessons are especially important today, as Christians face increasing opposition from the culture.

But, beyond reading the New Testament and some of the stories of early Christian martyrs, it can be daunting to figure out how to proceed. The complicated history of imperial Rome, along with the foreign names and locations featured in early Christian history, can make understanding the most important historical points difficult.

To uncover this fascinating history, we've produced our online course, "[Ancient Christianity](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z39jr03sh99bcq8erpvj5ng0ejj5ada16an58bo0_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdgeao72c9h2bs)," now available on DVD.

In this course, Professor Kenneth Calvert is your guide through the first four centuries of Christianity as he explains the remarkable ways the new faith transformed the Roman world.

Would you help support this course so we can make it available to millions of Americans for free? As a token of our appreciation for your support, we'll send you a copy of our beautiful DVD Box Set for any donations of $100 or more.
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zudq20p7qi7obgifer7m16ggr3febb5rqnl7l2pg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdgeao72c9h2bs)
**You can watch the video trailer for the course and get your copy of "Ancient Christianity" on DVD using the secure link below:**

[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2znocbdpukat40ltp497hl6s08ciqfo6456i0mdvg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdgeao72c9h2bs)**[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/ancient-christianity-dvd-trailer](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zjc1is7lub997gd6edt37hpransu521tpr1rt3mo_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdgeao72c9h2bs)**

Thank you for your support in reaching millions of Americans with the story of the courage and faith of the first Christians.

Best regards,

Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D.
Executive Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College

clean_body: These lessons are especially important today, as Christians face increasing opposition from the culture. This sponsored message from First Look is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | | | | Dear Friend, Learning about how early Christians worshipped and prayed, faced persecution and hardship, and settled theological disputes enriches our understanding of the faith. These lessons are especially important today, as Christians face increasing opposition from the culture. But, beyond reading the New Testament and some of the stories of early Christian martyrs, it can be daunting to figure out how to proceed. The complicated history of imperial Rome, along with the foreign names and locations featured in early Christian history, can make understanding the most important historical points difficult. To uncover this fascinating history, we've produced our online course, "," now available on DVD. In this course, Professor Kenneth Calvert is your guide through the first four centuries of Christianity as he explains the remarkable ways the new faith transformed the Roman world. Would you help support this course so we can make it available to millions of Americans for free? As a token of our appreciation for your support, we'll send you a copy of our beautiful DVD Box Set for any donations of $100 or more. You can watch the video trailer for the course and get your copy of "Ancient Christianity" on DVD using the secure link below: Thank you for your support in reaching millions of Americans with the story of the courage and faith of the first Christians. Best regards, Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D. Executive Director of Online Learning Hillsdale College | | Pop Acta Media | First Look News PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

name: Brigadier General James Emory Mace, for Nancy Mace for Governor
subject: I never thought I’d write something like this.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY NANCY MACE FOR GOVERNOR
body:
Please read what I have to say about my daughter

| | | [](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zicppmn1hd8ff10k94mv53rtm2mvtrjbea6068jo_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
| | [](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zkd0an8lfav9ovcv7fuodmr78iku07q809n5rt9g_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
|
---
| | Hi, This is Brigadier General James Emory Mace, U.S. Army (Retired). _**Today, I write to you not as a soldier... _but as a father._**_ I'm a **Purple Heart** veteran, and I’ve seen battle. I’ve worn the uniform. I’ve buried friends who never made it home. **And I’ve felt the sting of a Nation that sometimes forgets what our sacrifices were for.** _**[But my daughter—Congresswoman Nancy Mace—never forgot.](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z0p9e9mttnsjgneo7dqtuscr0ckn3jtl8qpoj4n8_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)**_ Nancy was raised in the shadow of the flag. She saw the uniform. She lived with the deployments. She knew the cost. _**And instead of running from it, she ran toward it.**_
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zerkm5d97f03207bscau74gfbfea9e75cdvnqr10_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
When Nancy was 17, she dropped out of high school. Most kids in that situation fall apart. But not Nancy.**She picked herself up, took a job at Waffle House, and fought her way back.** **By 21, she became the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s Military College. A place I knew well.**
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z37d2v0q894gp36r6phtbvfbdu7s0q6uf038m9b8_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
[ **SUPPORT NANCY MACE FOR GOVERNOR** ](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zanjgudqe2d4a09a0lv3ee19i91g5pjdda1t5nj8_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
**Nancy then made it to Congress, where _she represented the people of South Carolina’s First District._** She’s been fighting her whole life; for herself, for her kids, for the truth, and now...for her state. **[Today, Nancy is running for Governor of South Carolina. And just like she did at The Citadel, she’s marching headfirst into a battle.](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zi3fj0ni50dsiudqpe9lka49n2jdm352l9mfub3o_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)** It’s hard sitting back as a father and hearing the terrible things the media and the establishment say about her. [**_But Nancy is my daughter. And I know this: She never backs down. And she never stops fighting._**](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z9g8voh8maf54modp1tn5b6codqqapiitckn28fg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z9hetl0b8kaai4ti4dgda1k67h0i021b9dcnsl7o_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
**I want to ask you something from one parent, one patriot, to another:** [**_Will you support my daughter in the toughest battle of her life?_**](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zh8voo562t8vfeqlqd7nkinggb4o8r0t3i1ndnpo_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs) **She’s up against a machine that wants to __erase the values of faith-based, service-minded Americans who believe in truth, family, and country.__** Nancy stood up for me. She stood up for _**EVERY**_ veteran who was forgotten. She stood up when sanctuary sheriffs released illegal alien criminals onto South Carolina streets. She stood up when they tried to let biological men compete in women’s sports. She stood up when **other Republicans stayed silent.** **And now, she’s standing up for _YOU._** But she needs your backup. **_[**_I may not wear the uniform anymore,_but I still believe in answering the call.__**](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zortg0arn3o96k1i958afojbnkkvah63073n134g_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)___** Will you answer it with me today? **[Whether it’s $5, $10, or $15, your donation means everything right now.](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zm510posprbcblcvbjqnimecgdpl13rrv0lpmpe8_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)** Nancy’s not running to make friends. _**She’s running to _win._**_ **To fight. To serve.** ** ** That’s what she learned growing up in our home. That’s what she learned watching me go off to war. _**And that’s what she’ll carry with her to the Governor’s Mansion.**_ Please, on this solemn day, join me in standing with my daughter. [Yes, Nancy is a warrior.**But she is also my little girl—and she needs you, Patriot**](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z5sb2ss6lsedkk8iu90hsrhsa98e2qnguj972ng0_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
[ **DONATE TO NANCY'S CAMPAIGN** ](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z2810d851i4it3ufqebb08tu46dviki1mc74kgc8_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
With respect, General James Emory Mace (Ret.)_**Proud Father of Nancy Mace**_
---
| | [](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z26n96v23rt45jtk9m8s2jncv7f84cjg7d071tpo_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
| | [ **$25** ](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z9lna8ubi578fj75vbgucto6i88ql36frl4bqgoo_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
[ **$50** ](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zmbo7pe2eth3f39rs97k4dj7pi0cg42vrboa5ong_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
[ **$100** ](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z6srnbsg2omfb05oam4tr1e9m3058p86pbe846m8_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
[ **OTHER AMOUNT** ](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zinhr5qvn2btfeshskf5bbbit7lij0jpcc0g5t9g_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)
---
**High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.** **Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.** At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts. **By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch.** She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground.Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina. Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks. **She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.** Nancy is fighting to:
* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast
* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency
* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free
* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate
* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think
* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. **South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.**
---
| |
# PAID FOR BY NANCY MACE FOR GOVERNOR
---
| | 295 Seven Farms Drive, Suite C-186Charleston, SC 29492
---
---

Pop Acta Media | First Look News
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zsaku1fc3jbc1tfkfgj5fvjf64jt8o6pga9o2iho_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf9tge5rrasbjkcqb4tjhe6rrfdljkeqjetjnklprctbi6pr34t5i6krqfp9ls4o7et5hm6qbcsr7c0bjbd1km6pqcdtlmcibbp31c3j69p32c8gq48bi4bh28pr5clhm1p71cph649fg)_ | _[manage data preferences](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z9tr5hc6fburenidpk62c1qs0ormq8fmqg6gepcg_rp22sh2s8i6epb5cdge9ob6c9h2bs)_

[](http://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zti0a2sfbsjnrma83vsossui5edb2tq2oqu07gp0_rp2ash2s8j2ccdkmorb4dthe8gq48bi4bh28pr5clhm1p71cph649fg)

Nancy is fighting to: Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started. | | PAID FOR BY NANCY MACE FOR GOVERNOR | | 295 Seven Farms Drive, Suite C-186Charleston, SC 29492 Pop Acta Media | First Look News PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

Dear Citizen, We have great news to share! Doors are opening in Washington DC. Trump administration officials are listening. The media is hearing us. In July, we held a [press conference](https://d56tDQ04.na1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/UD+113/d56tDQ04/VVm7Rv6n0hrpW12DzRx1p97NMW7-75Mp5Bs13FN8NyyFK3m2nnW8wLKSR6lZ3l3W5CRxrD2d_8P9W3vvrPw1CkCfyW2WcwcC2H-kyGW3Kg0sp3Ly0kYW2MRjsQ3lGGq2W2T8kbz3z_7LpW3Fq7h04Sd1lnW2c6kVy5gX3nzW3rL-Px2HTf2SN8qcZ0lXMZ3yW5_n7341MkXdpVR9jnq8h1Jq6W3F-Ph_2rwWPgW5Jkv325Jq1XbW1Y9KSy8hg2FmW3B778s8XF3YHVmWbrR50DLgFW2cKgHH4HcQGZW2wwcTC8n90k7W94LRjf7cprVmVXrxsv823KSfW6l22DN3tgtqxW16f-nM5Ql6KyW12TpHj2G00dKW6lfC0N7QtDjRW3ZJrlN2wz4jSW8PR8T98ZG2rxW9cXP632RgFRwf9hyj3q04) at the National Press Club in Washington, DC to announce a new [filing](https://d56tDQ04.na1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/UD+113/d56tDQ04/VVm7Rv6n0hrpW12DzRx1p97NMW7-75Mp5Bs13FN8NyyDd5kBVzW5BWr2F6lZ3mKW3J4YMZ7SK7JqW6KNTRq2sz6kvW5DlqjG7mQRpXW6hJl7r316R3LW6CdsJW466s2SW4M9qxJ3PLc5fW6dP8BD5Lf-LhW5dZBzz8Rmrf2W7_40B18FZYnMW4JPm4D4zVdB9W5MyFwq5HfvqTW28P82K8p8zsmN8YJSZTFXQzyW3Z4Bsx5V9XqVW5728CM2sR4p5W80VZ-r3nVV3XW694LBz6-8X0GW38TGF81J6z4TW6B9Nfh1KK_tJW5bTrqj5lfZp7W5S70kZ5Gx090W1q1TSV1sFD5pW7cS8Cn18RQ66W1_qZRm5B4BqfN1b0r-ChNvlfW5lg7Xq5Y8BjpVcFsg93slrkyW5V2Kh62c6BHjN3RgDx7_n71HW1BwDM0936hzxW3JT1JZ2CQdz6W7q2bN-7J76CvW90vSX05nT-ZLW52299w7QpRB7f5-rCQK04) in our legal effort to hold election officials accountable in Colorado, both for invalid elections and for jailing whistle-blower Tina Peters. We also unveiled a new lawsuit to be filed against New York’s Attorney General Letitia James. More to come on that later.
---
|

---
|
---
There’s also the not-so-great news... Unite4Freedom has studied official records of certified federal elections in 32 states. Our team of 95 professional credentialed big data auditors produced comprehensive peer-reviewed reports for 2022 and 2024. The results speak to the urgency of our mission:
* America’s voter rolls have error rates as high as 48%. Tens of millions of votes are being counted from invalid registrations.
* Furthermore, we studied timestamped copies of the voter rolls in succession, as frequently as one hour apart, that demonstrated millions of individual edits to the totals that have **no** **legitimate** **explanation**! A minimum of 1.5 million edits were made to the **Texas** 2024 early voting history alone!
* Using multiple commercial identity verification and fraud prevention databases, we have discovered on average that 5% of voters whose votes were counted in 2024 **do not appear to exist at all.**

---
|
---
---
|
---
Your support has helped us tremendously in the past, and we humbly ask you to help us again. We need to buy voter participation lists and histories (yes, many states actually charge big money for those), conduct investigations in critical counties and states, and meet with Trump administration officials regularly. Together we will halt this brazen fraud on America!

name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
subject: I’m America Only
body:
Patriot, I’ve had enough of watching your hard-earned tax dollars
vanish into endless foreign wars and aid packages, while families back
home struggle to put food on the table.

American families are crumbling under crushing debt, and politicians
in DC act like it's all just fine - as long as their foreign lobbyist
friends are happy.

IT BREAKS MY HEART, AND IT SHOULD BREAK YOURS TOO. I've seen the pain
in the eyes of everyday Americans who've been left behind, forgotten
by a system that prioritizes everyone but us. That's why I refuse to
stay silent.
[https://secure.winred.com/marjorie-taylor-greene-s-people-over-politicians-c/mtg_don_wmb_er_aip-mtg_rs_20250820?recurring=true&utm_term=lrm-active]

AMERICA FIRST ISN'T SOME CATCHY PHRASE - IT'S A PROMISE I MAKE EVERY
DAY TO FIGHT FOR YOU, YOUR FAMILY, AND OUR FUTURE
[https://secure.winred.com/marjorie-taylor-greene-s-people-over-politicians-c/mtg_don_wmb_er_aip-mtg_rs_20250820?recurring=true&utm_term=lrm-active].

But it’s being watered down by "Republicans" who time and again put
foreign interests ahead of the interests of the people that elected
them; the people they claim to represent.

THAT’S WHY I’M EMBRACING A NEW MONIKER: AMERICA ONLY!
[https://secure.winred.com/marjorie-taylor-greene-s-people-over-politicians-c/mtg_don_wmb_er_aip-mtg_rs_20250820?recurring=true&utm_term=lrm-active]

SUPPORT AMERICA ONLY
[https://secure.winred.com/marjorie-taylor-greene-s-people-over-politicians-c/mtg_don_wmb_er_aip-mtg_rs_20250820?recurring=true&utm_term=lrm-active]

Friend, the powerful forces who profit from this betrayal are turning
their sights on me. AIPAC, the shadowy unregistered lobby pushing
Israel's agenda, is furious because I won't bow down.

THEY SEE ME AS A THREAT. A Congresswoman who can't be bought, who puts
faith in Jesus Christ above all, and who demands we stop the endless
giveaways that hollow out our nation.

NOW THEY'RE MOBILIZING THEIR DEEP-POCKETED DONORS TO SMEAR ME, SILENCE
ME, AND DEFEAT ME IN MY PRIMARY
[https://secure.winred.com/marjorie-taylor-greene-s-people-over-politicians-c/mtg_don_wmb_er_aip-mtg_rs_20250820?recurring=true&utm_term=lrm-active].

It enrages me, and it should enrage every patriot who loves this
country. We've sacrificed too much and are watching our dreams for our
children slip away while our leaders mortgage their future for foreign
powers.

I won't back down, but I can't do this alone. Your support has always
been my strength - the grassroots fire that keeps me going against the
odds.

_IF THIS INJUSTICE STIRS SOMETHING IN YOU, IF YOU'RE TIRED OF THE
SELLOUTS AND READY TO RECLAIM AMERICA, THEN DON’T WAIT FOR A
SECOND. _

TOGETHER, WE'LL SHOW THEM WHAT REAL AMERICAN RESOLVE LOOKS LIKE
[https://secure.winred.com/marjorie-taylor-greene-s-people-over-politicians-c/mtg_don_wmb_er_aip-mtg_rs_20250820?recurring=true&utm_term=lrm-active].

_For America,_

[https://secure.winred.com/marjorie-taylor-greene-s-people-over-politicians-c/mtg_don_wmb_er_aip-mtg_rs_20250820?recurring=true&utm_term=lrm-active]

MARJORIE TAYLOR GREENE

[https://secure.winred.com/marjorie-taylor-greene-s-people-over-politicians-c/mtg_don_wmb_er_aip-mtg_rs_20250820?recurring=true&utm_term=lrm-active]

# Wednesday, August 20, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1920, the [first commercial radio station in the United States](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO3CAQhJ8Gd5zWa2PjgiKNuz9tSouDxbcJhhOQs-7tI18e4e9GM9Knmfn1tX1Zjpz2n_Yg00LeEp01UmtUNu9cse6Pz2faSz5r4Tu9ydm2IaCSoCXC5i3H9zZ23gQipKEj089KTXrEZekeZqZlUkoH7_tlGugOSgdNlpYAFoKdOzYXDTRCDzCq5RaUnhCc60c7BEdejECeWy6335lTe1CI7_yicnP56KJ5tPasYvghcBW4FmolXxv-5wJXBASBK-iPFrgGLrVJl4-DimMbZbGes6zNNs5JcpJ_ZZV32jlVmZ9UbOO0X76_4NwErl0x_nlyjFynWYywH5bjp1D71osvg_8CAAD__5aqh4E), 8MK—now known as WWJ-AM—began operations in Detroit under the ownership of _The Detroit News_. Elton Plant, an office boy at the _News_ , became the first U.S. radio announcer, reaching perhaps 30 Detroit homes. 🎙️ In today’s _Flyover_ podcast, host Ayla Brown digs into what’s happening with the all-you-can-eat hotel breakfast. Once a travel staple, the buffet isn’t quite what it used to be. Why the shift? That’s a podcast-exclusive story you won’t see in the email edition. Listen now on [Spotify](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0MFOhDAQgOGnKbclw2xh4dCDBzkYF6PRGL2QbjsD1S4lpYHg05v1Fbz_-Q7_-7k_a-fdNHT6Sipx6CfaFk8pUeytMVGbbxu2aYhhW6K70E5Gpx4BywPUB4Teauf3XmZWMRHSMSNVnMqyqiU2TTaqCkBKNlafpG4uZLmRfGGutLbEZQGZUzcNaoQCQJZNzmVdIRhTSH1kQ1ZIIOtSiPlXcFMaif0eVoq5CdfMqzGleRHHO4GtwDbMNOXLHJLj_RYIbJcxbAJb2a0vD08__rnzw1v3-Pox3TfFZxaVnTfnvVuqk5AwXLXzf3L6145V4W8AAAD__5IEdRc), [YouTube](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO8yAQBOCnsTtbyxoSU1D8Tbr81UkpEVmWmDtiIkyC_Pan3D3CtTOar5jL2Z5dTHG9_Xd3NjVku3LbEtfKxXqi4ujL57beSm5biVfemVy1CKgGmAcE611Mu5W9N4EZeerZiKNSh1mi1v1ihCI1MWjlSU6aghBKC6kwCBSK-dhH89ZgRhAAUukxqPmAQCSkmwKx7ySwjzWX8TPHtS4c0p5fXEbK9z6ZpdbH1k3_Ojx1eGqtjXt-1ueV3_07lHBxlZbhY-Hh9Dvti_GPFlOK2-HYSbjdXUw_Xv3TCS-D3wEAAP__vopwTw), or [Apple Podcasts](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuwyAURb8Gb7bgGbA9MHTxlq4dLcx7xLTEWEBj5e-rVJXyAV3vvTpXOh-X5WJDDPv13d7IVJ-Wnc4SqVbKCzqXrfvCdO7XnM6Sw0oPcrYuwEG1fGyBL2hDfCyyQeOJgPqGjBiU0qOEaWo241ZS6zp4Ddp5KdSgAVecPDiSPa59E8yTxkfggnOpps6rUQN3Tkjbe0fIJCcMNeXuM4W9buTjI90pdy7dmmi2Wo_C-jcGM4P5SOhsqaWzxxHpOWEwf5dXw2CuG7V_jPaVBhSj4NDDpEWTDR5niDEUPTDJrzcb4u9f_Zeku4GfAAAA__9sunvM).
