That’s why I took JUUL and its former parent company Altria to court — and won a historic settlement to help repair the damage they caused. Just this week, Minnesota received an additional $2.5 million payment from JUUL. In total, JUUL and Altria have now paid over $41 million to Minnesota — dollars that are already going to work protecting kids and cutting underage tobacco and nicotine use in our state. It’s my job to stand up for Minnesotans when powerful corporations put profits before people. Wins like this don’t happen by accident — they happen because folks like you believe in justice, and you stand with me in this fight. But the work isn’t done. Too many corporations still think they can get away with preying on our kids and our communities. I need your help to prove them wrong. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thank you, Keith Ellison Attorney General Keith Ellison is the People’s Lawyer, fighting to help people afford their lives and live with safety, dignity, and respect. = | Prepared and paid for by the Keith Ellison for Attorney General committee, PO Box 17224, Minneapolis, MN 55417 Keith Ellison for Attorney General PO Box 17224 Minneapolis, MN 55417 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please . ************** ************ **

name: Brian Lenney
subject: Idaho Big Education Hands Over K-3 Reading to Artificial Intelligence
body:
Is this what you voted for?

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..jNK-L1xRRvUrxJANeWWbT9SKcm-nMjQrZSidRP7kBm4?) for more
---
# [Idaho Big Education Hands Over K-3 Reading to Artificial Intelligence ](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=171479003&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNDc5MDAzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTU3MDQ2NjYsImV4cCI6MTc1ODI5NjY2NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.v4hgNcOrIqMwvTe3exWXdIT7Tt4FF8zBB6enV0PIR7k)
### Is this what you voted for?
| | [Brian Lenney - Nampa's Senator](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---
| Aug 20
---
| [](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=171479003&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNDc5MDAzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NTcwNDY2NiwiZXhwIjoxNzU4Mjk2NjY2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjExNDgyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vF2j4gj_5PWB3uVC3-Ls2vlfx_B3Ebxu9GDNWLEqi-4&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=171479003&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNDc5MDAzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTU3MDQ2NjYsImV4cCI6MTc1ODI5NjY2NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.v4hgNcOrIqMwvTe3exWXdIT7Tt4FF8zBB6enV0PIR7k&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=171479003&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNDc5MDAzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTU3MDQ2NjYsImV4cCI6MTc1ODI5NjY2NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.v4hgNcOrIqMwvTe3exWXdIT7Tt4FF8zBB6enV0PIR7k)
---
| | [](https://substack..BsrlZkYciRGjSBT6zCd8SQv4ehM2dMJB-EmK6snWneU?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/brianlenney/p/idaho-big-education-hands-over-k?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3693307f-fb5d-4cb7-b071-2ba409c808b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Earlier this year, the Idaho Department of Education, led by Superintendent Debbie Critchfield, [doubled down on outsourcing one of the state's most critical educational responsibilities](https://substack.com/redirect/b7fedbd0-b5eb-4d9b-8fde-e30acf62fdac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to artificial intelligence (instead of human teachers).

The 2025-26 Idaho Reading Indicator (IRI), a high-stakes assessment mandated by [Idaho Code 33-1806](https://substack.com/redirect/c2b252f7-a9d2-496a-9769-b7a41c6d5e59?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for all public school students in kindergarten through third grade, is now in the hands of a San Francisco company called "[Amira Learning.](https://substack.com/redirect/e8b70ce7-89ff-4e7d-9d1c-3013f13e7d32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"

This places **San Francisco artificial intelligence** at the heart of assessing and guiding young readers in Idaho, _not their teachers._
## Rise of the Machines
The Idaho Reading Indicator is administered twice yearly and has been a cornerstone of Idaho's efforts to ensure kids can read by third grade.
Since 2016, Istation's Indicators of Progress (ISIP) has been the backbone of the IRI, a computer-adaptive test that adjusts question difficulty based on student responses. But the 2025 iteration, powered by Amira's A.I., takes things to a whole new level.
According to Amira Learning, their system "listens as students read aloud, using advanced speech recognition and natural language processing to continuously assess reading mastery and inform practice and instruction, powering true differentiation."
> #### Translation: kids as young as five are reading to a machine, and that machine, not a teacher, decides how well they're doing and what they need next.
Amira's A.I. apparently identifies reading difficulties in real time, delivers "micro-interventions" based on decades of reading and neuroscience research, and even targets support for students with dyslexia.
But this isn't about supplementing human teaching. _It's about replacing it._
## Teachers & Students Left Behind
Teaching kids to read is one of the most human endeavors in education.
It requires patience, intuition, and the ability to connect with a child's unique needs. These are things no algorithm can replicate.
Yet Idaho's decision to lean on artificial intelligence means that K-3 students are reading aloud to a computer, not a teacher who can notice a furrowed brow, a hesitant pause, or that moment when it all clicks.
The system claims to "minimize stress" and "maximize instructional time," but what does it mean for a kindergartener to perform for a faceless robot instead of a trusted adult?
And what about the teachers, relegated to interpreting web-based reports rather than actively engaging with their students' reading journeys?
The IRI determines whether kids are at grade level (Tier 1), near or below grade level (Tier 2), or well below grade level (Tier 3). And these tiers trigger interventions that can shape a child's educational path _**for years**_.
If A.I. misjudges a student's ability (e.g. due to a speech impediment, background noise) kids could be mislabeled, misdirected, or left behind. And we've seen glitches before: in 2023 (my first year in the Idaho legislature), "Istation" (now "Amira") [delivered inaccurate data affecting over 90% of Idaho's districts and charters, delaying spring IRI results by nearly four months](https://substack.com/redirect/3067dd96-6e68-416d-b8c2-6407b3aacb9d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)!
Look at this:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5d752474-0f43-460d-a046-4882c53c6c12?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9da5faaa-03cf-45ac-9497-d1e144fff2df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/abb23c0a-1cb9-45ad-bac6-074b56e5cc49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5ab86928-28f2-4f6e-906d-5da3a2f4ec07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Crazy, right?
_**Yet, despite that track record, Idaho renewed its faith in Istation/Amira for up to five more years!**_
The decision to stick with Istation/Amira wasn't a foregone conclusion. Or, was it?
The Idaho Department of Education sought bids to replace the expiring IRI contract, claiming to have gathered [input from nearly 2,000 stakeholders via a survey](https://substack.com/redirect/de7a4604-d998-4520-87fa-3a4021677ce2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
But whispers in some education circles suggest the process was anything but open. Allegations are swirling that certain organizations were prevented from responding to the RFP, effectively rigging the game in favor of Istation/Amira.
1. In 2016, [Istation was the low bidder at $64,350](https://substack.com/redirect/83b75e4c-38f5-4b2d-913d-c8b50e2efb19?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for a pilot program.
2. By 2023-24, the cost had ballooned to $724,000 (**+1,024.8% increase**), and with the new five-year contract, taxpayers can expect to foot an even heftier bill.
3. Meanwhile, other vendors like _Curriculum Associates_ , _Renaissance_ , and _Cambium_([some of whom are on the State Board's approved literacy intervention list](https://substack.com/redirect/7586b9b8-855a-4e62-b170-21d61a8eedf2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) were seemingly [shut out from competing](https://substack.com/redirect/416bf89a-48c9-42d9-9083-f5f4539b611d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

Why?
Was it to push Amira's shiny new A.I. as the future of Idaho education? We're left with more questions than answers.
This A.I. pivot comes at a time when Idaho's reading scores are in the toilet.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c4255440-c714-49a3-b453-9f3c1cb6d4e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The state's investment in literacy, ($5M for phonics-based teacher training in 2025 for instance), shows a commitment to evidence-based practices like the Science of Reading and that is a GOOD THING!
So handing the IRI to A.I. feels like a contradiction, doesn't it?
Because _The Science of Reading_ emphasizes human-led, explicit instruction in phonics, not automated interventions. To be fair, studies, [like this one from Johns Hopkins](https://substack.com/redirect/97d5050f-74d1-4947-ab22-7afa4bdd9d27?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2020, show Istation's ISIP correlates with reading gains... _**but only when paired with teacher-driven instruction!**_
A.I. isn't the silver bullet it's being sold as.
Not all kids have equal access to the tech needed for Istation's browser-based or app-dependent platform, especially in rural Idaho. And while Amira touts "linguistically authentic and culturally responsive" assessments, there's no guarantee its algorithms are free from bias, particularly for English learners or students with disabilities (yeah, I went there).
[The 2020-21 Idaho Literacy Intervention Program Evaluation](https://substack.com/redirect/3c798b91-7f7b-4641-9727-7b71e0c11895?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) noted that the covid scamdemic already disrupted learning, with some schools struggling to administer the IRI due to tech limitations.
So betting on A.I. in this context feels like putting a band-aid on a broken system.
#### **Bottom-line: Idaho kids deserve better than to have their reading skills judged by a machine, especially one backed by a company with a history of data errors and a shady selection process.**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/43c6b42d-4367-4b61-b655-2bc78e95c6de?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Debbie Critchfield's decision to entrust the IRI to Amira Learning smacks of prioritizing flashy tech over proven pedagogy.
**Teachers, parents, and taxpayers should demand answers** :
1. Were other vendors sidelined?
2. Why are we spending millions to let A.I. teach our kids to read when human educators, armed with the Science of Reading, could do it better?

If we're serious about literacy, we need to invest in teachers, not algorithms.
We need transparency, not secret deals. And we need to trust the humans in the classroom, not the machines in the cloud. Until then, Idaho's Big Education experiment with A.I. is a gamble we can't afford to lose.
Idaho kids' futures are on the line.
Let's make sure they're in human hands.

---

[](https://substack.com/redirect/a11e8322-359d-460a-8119-a161490a5d18?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[](https://substack..YqlwDozQgvnJ356RgKHVji8SmLcDptcKufbNk0F538w?)

name: Tina Kotek
subject: I’m really proud of this legislation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek
body:
|
#
---
Peter — I’m really proud to have signed these bills into law. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113664544/564535640/-773863328?refcode=AU_TK_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250820_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNThiM2Y3ZC1kYzdkLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRhOWQyM2UiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gMKRTpoJPnInDw6z2Q8mk2rk5GfiM6BsyXX5ilplCKw=) Last week, I signed a series of bipartisan bills sent to my desk that will improve and expand mental health and addiction care statewide. Mental health struggles and the disease of addiction don’t discriminate – they touch the lives of Oregonians from all backgrounds. These investments build the capacity of our mental health system and give providers more tools to intervene when someone needs help. Tackling this crisis requires a comprehensive approach that supports people in crisis, the social workers and counselors who are on the ground and doing the work, and builds a long-term strategy to address the addiction crisis. **[These are the sensible solutions we’re fighting for, Peter. clean_body: | Peter — I’m really proud to have signed these bills into law. = Last week, I signed a series of bipartisan bills sent to my desk that will improve and expand mental health and addiction care statewide. Mental health struggles and the disease of addiction don’t discriminate – they touch the lives of Oregonians from all backgrounds. These investments build the capacity of our mental health system and give providers more tools to intervene when someone needs help. Tackling this crisis requires a comprehensive approach that supports people in crisis, the social workers and counselors who are on the ground and doing the work, and builds a long-term strategy to address the addiction crisis. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Something we can all agree on: help and care should be there when you need it, no matter where you live or what you can afford. Thanks for your support, Tina Tina for Oregon PO Box 42307 Portland, OR 97242 | mailto:info@tinafororegon.com | Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek

| | Hi Friend, When I came to Congress, I came to fight for the people who raised me, stood with me, and taught me what community means. That fight takes all of us, every day, in every way we can show up. Right now, we’re halfway through August, and we’re falling short of our mid-month fundraising target. I’m not telling you this to alarm you — I’m telling you because this is how grassroots movements stay strong. The reality is, the same forces working to roll back health care, gut food assistance, and erode our civil rights are gearing up for 2026. They want our voices quieter. **But if we meet this midmonth goal, we’ll have the resources to knock on more doors, organize more neighbors, and keep our district — and our values — at the center of the fight.** [**If you can, help us raise $5,000 before tomorrow at midnight so we can get back on track as we head into the fall.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113664631/564535833/-545973753?&refcode=E14699-20250820-MidMonth1-SL-DeadlineOther&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQ0NDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWIwODNlYjQtZGM3ZC1mMDExLWI0ODEtNjA0NWJkYTlkMjNlIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=v-PDkeJO4YPUcvhppKb9Q26QaXbhj8GaIucZjQiobUM=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [**CHIP IN $5 NOW**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113664632/564535834/-1419634142?refcode=E14699-20250820-MidMonth1-grid-DeadlineOther&amount=5&express_lane=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQ0NDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWIwODNlYjQtZGM3ZC1mMDExLWI0ODEtNjA0NWJkYTlkMjNlIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=v-PDkeJO4YPUcvhppKb9Q26QaXbhj8GaIucZjQiobUM=)

Lateefah Simon has spent her career fighting for justice—fighting for folks without a voice. Now, as a 25-year veteran organizer and nationally recognized advocate for civil rights and social justice, she’s fighting this fight from the Capitol. In Lateefah, CA-12 has a proven champion. To learn more about Lateefah, visit: LateefahSimon.com Emails like this are the most effective way to grow our campaign and reach supporters like you. clean_body: Midmonth check-in: where we stand, and how we win. | = = | | Hi Friend, When I came to Congress, I came to fight for the people who raised me, stood with me, and taught me what community means. That fight takes all of us, every day, in every way we can show up. Right now, we’re halfway through August, and we’re falling short of our mid-month fundraising target. I’m not telling you this to alarm you — I’m telling you because this is how grassroots movements stay strong. The reality is, the same forces working to roll back health care, gut food assistance, and erode our civil rights are gearing up for 2026. They want our voices quieter. But if we meet this midmonth goal, we’ll have the resources to knock on more doors, organize more neighbors, and keep our district — and our values — at the center of the fight. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = = | = = | = = This campaign is fueled by people, not PACs or special interests. And when people lead, we win. In gratitude, Lateefah | * | | ---|---|--- Lateefah Simon for Congress 1714 Franklin St #100 - 438 Oakland, CA 94612 mailto:info@lateefahsimon.com = = Lateefah Simon has spent her career fighting for justice—fighting for folks without a voice. name: Team Kelly Morrison
subject: Sneak peek at our MN State Fair booth! 👀🎡
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Morrison for Congress
body:
https://kellyforcongress.org [https://kellyforcongress.org]
Hi Peter!
Team Kelly here. We are so excited to kick off the Minnesota State Fair tomorrow morning! In case you missed it, Kelly’s campaign for Congress has its own booth this year! We have had many incredible volunteers put in so much time to make this booth a success – including a handful of Carpenters Union members, who woke up early and helped assemble our booth in our spot!
Photo of union carpenters constructing the state fair booth. [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP59/1/109390/images/places/state-fair-booth.jpg]
We hope you’ll swing by and visit us (across the road from the DNR building) and chat with our amazing volunteers, grab a postcard, and hear more about our campaign and what Kelly is doing to fight back in Washington.
Peter, with an average of 2 million fairgoers each year, the Great Minnesota Get Together will be an excellent way to meet with ALL Minnesotans to get the word out about our campaign. But in order to keep up with the expenses of the Fair, we started the ‘Fund the Fair’ campaign earlier this summer to close our budget gaps. This fund has helped cover the supplies needed to prepare our booth, campaign postcards to pass out, and Fair tickets for our dedicated volunteers and team.
Now, with less than 24 hours until the start of the Fair, we just pulled our ‘Fund the Fair’ numbers and realized we are just over $2,600 away from our goal.
Peter, could you chip in any amount today to help us close this gap before the Fair kicks off tomorrow at 7 AM? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fundthefair2025-email]
FUND THE FAIR [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fundthefair2025-email]
Thank you so much for all your support, Peter. We hope to see you at the Fair!
With gratitude,
Team Kelly
https://www.facebook.com/KellyMorrisonForCongress [https://www.facebook.com/KellyMorrisonForCongress] https://twitter.com/Morrison4MN [https://twitter.com/Morrison4MN] https://instagram.com/kellymorrisonforcongress [https://instagram.com/kellymorrisonforcongress] kellyforcongress.org [kellyforcongress.org]
A quick note from Kelly:
I've spent most of my career as a doctor, not a politician, and I believe we need leaders who are focused on delivering results for Minnesota families. I'm not taking a cent of corporate PAC or federal lobbyist money , so I'm counting on folks like your giving what they can, when they can. Will you chip in $10 or whatever you can today?
Donate $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/morrison-email?refcode2=emailfooter&amount=10&type=rbtn]
Email is the best way to keep our supporters up to date.
If you only want to receive our most important emails, click here to receive fewer emails. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/hHyPtKcXyUeK_xcY7BEsAQ2]
We’d hate to see you go, but if you want you can unsubscribe here [https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/4Dh5T9ni_kaWmPt-auzpZw2?unsubscribedata=mBYrBSuUglhPfv1ZCZZc9MKD31IfG4T%2Fiz9niy0BK%2Be2IU1akg5iM1ybYAndnn1FMRTq55jmBDKJyMdAjZ%2FD7CqB98zENDvNErUx3zc9U8F5qv5RLf5axKomADgLfLzuAREVZaMfWf0lO%2FbARwEknxkTWz0GSLQ0%2BH4lbcBGfE9%2FfFCj8Vh%2FWiHEGhG8lBPOv183H5BuUUTZAdq2zsUcQNLUfwPJfV7LCyEdOQwu%2B7cjXz2brsXhVbUIBpYV8mYo9Q1F9y1wtFlMEYd4pSfRWp46hLAXk6YrwO17FfNzTjzCVwb6kYcVRtj12ajwUH%2BspUfmgMhdX5RcgZa%2Bor%2BCoGw3MRC8bpCQNs%2FxI55guHCH6wtkdBwWQT6yC0O5ucVHwY00yj1%2BWXP5k7n7%2BWnL3r5Hc6hbKfp5ZfwWDmieSiJ1xnQ%2BgDE4Jx5EIGGSE0rt] .

# [Why Americans Must Speak Up for Gaza, South Sudan, and the Congo--Even as Democracy Falters at Home](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=283449&post_id=171483125&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=false&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNDgzMTI1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTU3MDYxNjEsImV4cCI6MTc1ODI5ODE2MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4MzQ0OSIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ._7NwMxdobuV2TSJh-p5Ouw-ChFvAwTS8Y87gKyTbWrg)
### A moral commentary on my news feature from earlier today
Aug 20

_Help me keep this newsletter free by signing up for a paid subscription!_
[ Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vYWNjb3VudCIsInAiOjE3MTQ4MzEyNSwicyI6MjgzNDQ5LCJmIjpmYWxzZSwidSI6OTA1MDExOTIsImlhdCI6MTc1NTcwNjE2MSwiZXhwIjoyMDcxMjgyMTYxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMCIsInN1YiI6ImxpbmstcmVkaXJlY3QifQ.9VoJ7mq_XF2CynF6TmdtgyPbCy7wYAmcrvTPydeLG7Y?)
## The Shrinking Gaze
I published an article earlier today about some of the most critical crises in the world. I received several comments asking me why I talked so much about crises overseas when American itself is unraveling.
In an era when American democracy itself seems fragile -- when voting maps erase voices, courts roll back rights, and authoritarian impulses seep from the margins into the mainstream -- it is tempting to shrink our gaze. To imagine that the only task left is to shore up the leaking foundations of our own house.
Democracy itself is wobbling here in the United States. In 2022, the Supreme Court overturned _Roe v. Wade_ , erasing a half-century of precedent and signaling the fragility of established rights when subjected to partisan courts.
In 2023, extreme gerrymandering in states like Alabama and North Carolina dismantled the voting power of Black and young voters, even after federal courts declared them unconstitutional.
Since 2020, more than 360 restrictive voting bills have been introduced in state legislatures, with at least 17 states enacting new laws to make access to the ballot harder. Meanwhile, election workers across the country face harassment and death threats simply for counting votes.
The metrics echo the unease: Freedom House now ranks the U.S. a "flawed democracy," pointing to declining public trust, partisan manipulation of electoral maps, and the spread of political violence.
The geography of democracy's decline is clear: from Tallahassee to Topeka, Austin to Atlanta, the map is dotted with legislatures where majority rule is being systematically diluted. This is the storm we stand in, and it is precisely why raising our voices about Gaza, South Sudan, and the Congo matters. Democracy is not only about what happens inside our borders--it is about refusing silence wherever power crushes the vulnerable.
Even as the American experiment teeters, we cannot afford to wall ourselves off from the wider world. Our consumption, our foreign policy, and even our silence ripple outward.
Gaza, South Sudan, and the Democratic Republic of Congo are not abstractions, not distant tragedies unfolding in parallel universes. They are sites where American power, American weapons, and American demand intersect with the most vulnerable people on Earth.
To ignore them is not just to betray others; it is to corrode the very democratic values we claim to defend at home.
* * *
## Gaza: Where Silence Kills
In Gaza, more than 200,000 Palestinians have been killed since October 2023. Nearly 70% of the dead are women and children. Another 1.7 million--three-quarters of the enclave's entire population -- have been displaced, crammed into makeshift tents or overcrowded U.N. shelters.
The health system has collapsed. By mid-2024, the World Health Organization reported that only 13 of Gaza's 36 hospitals were even partially functional. Drinking water has grown scarce: UNICEF estimates that 90% of Gaza's water supply is unfit for human consumption. The U.N. warned in early 2025 that "full-scale famine" could arrive within months if aid convoys continue to be blocked.
This is not a conflict sealed off by borders. U.S.-manufactured munitions have been recovered in the rubble of schools and hospitals. American tax dollars subsidize the very bombs that fall. In the United States, to remain silent on the genocide is not neutrality--it is complicity in the machinery of destruction.
> **Genocide in Gaza**
> **200,000+ killed** since October 2023, with women and children making up nearly 70% of the dead (U.N., 2025).
> **1.7 million displaced** -- three-quarters of the population forced from their homes (OCHA, 2025).
> **90% of water undrinkable** , leaving families dependent on unsafe or trucked-in supplies (UNICEF, 2024).
> **" Imminent famine"** warning issued by the U.N. as food convoys face repeated blockades (WFP, 2025).
> **Billions in U.S. military aid** flow to Israel annually, with U.S.-made bombs recovered in the rubble of schools and hospitals (U.S. State Dept., 2024).
* * *
## South Sudan: Climate at the Breaking Point
South Sudan, the world's youngest country, emerged from decades of civil war in 2011 with the fragile hope of peace. Yet in 2025, it is one of the most climate-vulnerable nations on Earth.
Over 9 million people--three-quarters of its population--require humanitarian assistance. Floods, intensified by climate change, have submerged entire swaths of Jonglei, Unity, and Upper Nile states. Since 2020, more than 2 million people have been displaced by rising waters. At the same time, drought cycles have grown more severe, pitting herding communities against one another in violent clashes over dwindling pasture and water.
Food insecurity is catastrophic. The World Food Program reports that 7.1 million South Sudanese face "crisis" or worse levels of hunger in 2025. Nearly 1.6 million children under five are acutely malnourished. These numbers are not the accidents of geography--they are the fingerprints of a destabilizing climate system to which the United States remains one of the largest contributors.
* * *
> **Climate and Conflict in South Sudan**
> **9 million people** in need of aid (OCHA, 2025)
> **2 million displaced** by flooding since 2020 (UNHCR, 2024)
> **7.1 million food insecure** , with 1.6 million children malnourished (WFP, 2025)
> **More than 400,000 killed** in civil conflict since 2013 (Council on Foreign Relations, 2023)
* * *
## The Democratic Republic of Congo: The Cost of Our Devices
If Gaza is the site where American bombs fall and South Sudan where American emissions haunt, then the Democratic Republic of Congo is where American consumption takes its purest extractive form.
The DRC is the world's largest source of cobalt--supplying over 70% of the global market--and a key producer of copper and coltan, minerals essential to smartphones, laptops, and the batteries driving the clean-energy revolution. But the cost is staggering.
UNICEF estimates that 40,000 children work in DRC's mines, exposed to toxic dust and the constant risk of tunnel collapse. Armed groups finance their wars through mineral extraction, fueling a cycle of violence that has displaced over 7 million people in the country's east. The U.N. reports that more than 25 million Congolese need humanitarian aid in 2025, making it one of the largest and most neglected crises on Earth.
When Americans tap on smartphones or plug in electric vehicles, the DRC is present in every flicker of the screen and every mile driven. Geography doesn't let us escape responsibility; it insists we recognize our complicity.
* * *
> **Cobalt and Smartphones
>
> 70% of global cobalt** comes from the DRC (USGS, 2024)
> **40,000 children** estimated in cobalt mines (Amnesty International, 2023)
> **7 million displaced** by armed conflict (OCHA, 2025)
> **25 million in need** of humanitarian assistance (U.N., 2025)
* * *
## Why Speaking Matters
It can feel indulgent, even futile, to speak of foreign suffering while democracy itself falters here at home. But the act of speaking--the refusal to let brutality become background noise--is itself democratic practice.
When authoritarianism tightens its grip domestically, silence becomes easier. We grow accustomed to swallowing outrage, to reserving our voices for only the most local of fights. But democracy depends on the opposite instinct: on the willingness to name injustice wherever it occurs, and to demand accountability from those in power.
A map is not only a representation of land but of responsibility. Rivers cross borders, carbon knows no passport, and weapons carry their manufacturer's marks across oceans.
To speak up for Gaza, South Sudan, and the DRC is to remember that democracy is not just the machinery of elections. It is the chorus of citizens insisting--loudly, imperfectly, but insistently--that every human life has value.
* * *
> **American Links to Global Crises**
> **Weapons** : U.S. provides $3.8 billion annually in military aid to Israel, much of it used in Gaza (U.S. State Department, 2024).
> **Emissions** : The U.S. remains the world's second-largest carbon emitter, directly tied to climate impacts like South Sudan's floods (Global Carbon Project, 2024).
> **Consumption** : Each smartphone sold in the U.S. contains cobalt mined in the DRC. Apple, Tesla, and others source from Congolese supply chains (Amnesty International, 2023).
* * *
The crises in Gaza, South Sudan, and the Congo are not foreign -- they are bound up with our policies, our consumption, our silence. To speak for those who cannot is not a distraction from the fight for democracy in America; it is a fulfillment of it.
Democracy is not only a matter of ballots cast but of voices raised. In refusing to go silent, we resist not only foreign brutality but also the corrosion of our own civic life. And in that act of solidarity, across oceans and fault lines, we may keep alive the possibility that democracy--here and everywhere--still has a chance.

A moral commentary on my news feature from earlier today Aug 20 | | | | | | | | | | | Help me keep this newsletter free by signing up for a paid subscription! ? The Shrinking Gaze I published an article earlier today about some of the most critical crises in the world. I received several comments asking me why I talked so much about crises overseas when American itself is unraveling. In an era when American democracy itself seems fragile -- when voting maps erase voices, courts roll back rights, and authoritarian impulses seep from the margins into the mainstream -- it is tempting to shrink our gaze. To imagine that the only task left is to shore up the leaking foundations of our own house. Democracy itself is wobbling here in the United States. In 2022, the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade , erasing a half-century of precedent and signaling the fragility of established rights when subjected to partisan courts. In 2023, extreme gerrymandering in states like Alabama and North Carolina dismantled the voting power of Black and young voters, even after federal courts declared them unconstitutional. Since 2020, more than 360 restrictive voting bills have been introduced in state legislatures, with at least 17 states enacting new laws to make access to the ballot harder. Meanwhile, election workers across the country face harassment and death threats simply for counting votes. The metrics echo the unease: Freedom House now ranks the U.S. a "flawed democracy," pointing to declining public trust, partisan manipulation of electoral maps, and the spread of political violence. The geography of democracy's decline is clear: from Tallahassee to Topeka, Austin to Atlanta, the map is dotted with legislatures where majority rule is being systematically diluted. This is the storm we stand in, and it is precisely why raising our voices about Gaza, South Sudan, and the Congo matters. Democracy is not only about what happens inside our borders--it is about refusing silence wherever power crushes the vulnerable. Even as the American experiment teeters, we cannot afford to wall ourselves off from the wider world. Our consumption, our foreign policy, and even our silence ripple outward. Gaza, South Sudan, and the Democratic Republic of Congo are not abstractions, not distant tragedies unfolding in parallel universes. They are sites where American power, American weapons, and American demand intersect with the most vulnerable people on Earth. To ignore them is not just to betray others; it is to corrode the very democratic values we claim to defend at home. Gaza: Where Silence Kills In Gaza, more than 200,000 Palestinians have been killed since October 2023. Nearly 70% of the dead are women and children. Another 1.7 million--three-quarters of the enclave's entire population -- have been displaced, crammed into makeshift tents or overcrowded U.N. shelters. The health system has collapsed. By mid-2024, the World Health Organization reported that only 13 of Gaza's 36 hospitals were even partially functional. Drinking water has grown scarce: UNICEF estimates that 90% of Gaza's water supply is unfit for human consumption. The U.N. warned in early 2025 that "full-scale famine" could arrive within months if aid convoys continue to be blocked. This is not a conflict sealed off by borders. U.S.-manufactured munitions have been recovered in the rubble of schools and hospitals. American tax dollars subsidize the very bombs that fall. In the United States, to remain silent on the genocide is not neutrality--it is complicity in the machinery of destruction. Genocide in Gaza 200,000+ killed since October 2023, with women and children making up nearly 70% of the dead (U.N., 2025). 1.7 million displaced -- three-quarters of the population forced from their homes (OCHA, 2025). 90% of water undrinkable , leaving families dependent on unsafe or trucked-in supplies (UNICEF, 2024). " Imminent famine" warning issued by the U.N. as food convoys face repeated blockades (WFP, 2025). Billions in U.S. military aid flow to Israel annually, with U.S.-made bombs recovered in the rubble of schools and hospitals (U.S. State Dept., 2024). South Sudan: Climate at the Breaking Point South Sudan, the world's youngest country, emerged from decades of civil war in 2011 with the fragile hope of peace. Yet in 2025, it is one of the most climate-vulnerable nations on Earth. Over 9 million people--three-quarters of its population--require humanitarian assistance. Floods, intensified by climate change, have submerged entire swaths of Jonglei, Unity, and Upper Nile states. Since 2020, more than 2 million people have been displaced by rising waters. At the same time, drought cycles have grown more severe, pitting herding communities against one another in violent clashes over dwindling pasture and water. Food insecurity is catastrophic. The World Food Program reports that 7.1 million South Sudanese face "crisis" or worse levels of hunger in 2025. Nearly 1.6 million children under five are acutely malnourished. These numbers are not the accidents of geography--they are the fingerprints of a destabilizing climate system to which the United States remains one of the largest contributors. Climate and Conflict in South Sudan 9 million people in need of aid (OCHA, 2025) 2 million displaced by flooding since 2020 (UNHCR, 2024) 7.1 million food insecure , with 1.6 million children malnourished (WFP, 2025) More than 400,000 killed in civil conflict since 2013 (Council on Foreign Relations, 2023) The Democratic Republic of Congo: The Cost of Our Devices If Gaza is the site where American bombs fall and South Sudan where American emissions haunt, then the Democratic Republic of Congo is where American consumption takes its purest extractive form. The DRC is the world's largest source of cobalt--supplying over 70% of the global market--and a key producer of copper and coltan, minerals essential to smartphones, laptops, and the batteries driving the clean-energy revolution. But the cost is staggering. UNICEF estimates that 40,000 children work in DRC's mines, exposed to toxic dust and the constant risk of tunnel collapse. Armed groups finance their wars through mineral extraction, fueling a cycle of violence that has displaced over 7 million people in the country's east. The U.N. reports that more than 25 million Congolese need humanitarian aid in 2025, making it one of the largest and most neglected crises on Earth. When Americans tap on smartphones or plug in electric vehicles, the DRC is present in every flicker of the screen and every mile driven. Geography doesn't let us escape responsibility; it insists we recognize our complicity. Cobalt and Smartphones 70% of global cobalt comes from the DRC (USGS, 2024) 40,000 children estimated in cobalt mines (Amnesty International, 2023) 7 million displaced by armed conflict (OCHA, 2025) 25 million in need of humanitarian assistance (U.N., 2025) Why Speaking Matters It can feel indulgent, even futile, to speak of foreign suffering while democracy itself falters here at home. But the act of speaking--the refusal to let brutality become background noise--is itself democratic practice. When authoritarianism tightens its grip domestically, silence becomes easier. We grow accustomed to swallowing outrage, to reserving our voices for only the most local of fights. But democracy depends on the opposite instinct: on the willingness to name injustice wherever it occurs, and to demand accountability from those in power. A map is not only a representation of land but of responsibility. Rivers cross borders, carbon knows no passport, and weapons carry their manufacturer's marks across oceans. To speak up for Gaza, South Sudan, and the DRC is to remember that democracy is not just the machinery of elections. It is the chorus of citizens insisting--loudly, imperfectly, but insistently--that every human life has value. American Links to Global Crises Weapons : U.S. provides $3.8 billion annually in military aid to Israel, much of it used in Gaza (U.S. State Department, 2024). Emissions : The U.S. remains the world's second-largest carbon emitter, directly tied to climate impacts like South Sudan's floods (Global Carbon Project, 2024). Consumption : Each smartphone sold in the U.S. contains cobalt mined in the DRC. Apple, Tesla, and others source from Congolese supply chains (Amnesty International, 2023). The crises in Gaza, South Sudan, and the Congo are not foreign -- they are bound up with our policies, our consumption, our silence. To speak for those who cannot is not a distraction from the fight for democracy in America; it is a fulfillment of it. Democracy is not only a matter of ballots cast but of voices raised. In refusing to go silent, we resist not only foreign brutality but also the corrosion of our own civic life. And in that act of solidarity, across oceans and fault lines, we may keep alive the possibility that democracy--here and everywhere--still has a chance. ? References United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA). Gaza Humanitarian Emergency Updates. (2024-2025). World Health Organization. Gaza Health Crisis Reports. (2024). United Nations World Food Programme. South Sudan Situation Report. (2024). UNICEF. Child Displacement and Hunger in South Sudan. (2023-2024). UNHCR. Displacement Data for South Sudan. (2024). United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA). Democratic Republic of Congo Humanitarian Response Plan. (2025). Amnesty International. Child Labor in Cobalt Mines of the DRC. (2023). U.S. Department of State. Security and Human Rights in the DRC. (2024). McKibben, B. (2021). The Flag, the Cross, and the Station Wagon. clean_body: , I'm writing to you today as a big supporter of Winsome Earle-Sears. As the current Lt. Governor of Virginia, Winsome 's story has already inspired kids of all races from all backgrounds. But now, her race for Governor is TIED, and she urgently needs our help to take the lead. As a conservative Christian, I know that Winsome shares our values and will fight tirelessly to protect our families and our freedoms. But despite her impressive record, she's getting outraised MASSIVELY on the fundraising front by her liberal opponent. That's why I'm counting on you to join me in supporting Winsome today. We're not just fighting for bragging rights - we're on a mission to lift families up and create a better future for our kids. But we can't do it without your help. Winsome is up against a MAJOR deadline, and she needs your urgent support to stay competitive. opponent is doubling her fundraising numbers thanks to wealthy megadonors like George Soros. If we don't act now, we risk losing the fight for the values that make our country great. But with your help, I know we can make a difference and elect a true conservative champion like Winsome Earle-Sears. Sincerely,. Sarah Huckabee Sanders . Marine. Mom. Small Business Owner. body:
This isn’t politics as usual.
---
Derek, We’re about to ask you to **[donate directly to the DNC today.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250820_4381_fr&amount=10&refcode2=4381_22930333_d2Gu6I&akid=4381%2E22930333%2Ed2Gu6I)** If you give us a moment of your time, we’ll explain why your support is so important right now. Here’s the situation: Republicans want to rig the 2026 election to make sure they control Congress — regardless of how the people vote. **This isn’t politics as usual. It’s an emergency for our democracy. And we can’t just stand by and let Trump burn everything down.** Democrats are fighting fire with fire. In California, Governor Gavin Newsom just called for a statewide ballot measure to counter Republicans’ gerrymandering efforts in Texas. This new measure would level the playing field and help make sure voters decide who runs the House of Representatives — _not Donald Trump._ But the truth is, this fight isn’t going to be easy. If this measure is on the ballot, Republicans will spend whatever it takes to stop it from succeeding. **[That’s why we’re asking for your help. Can you chip in a $3 donation today and help fund Democrats’ efforts to protect our democracy?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250820_4381_fr&amount=3&refcode2=4381_22930333_d2Gu6I&akid=4381%2E22930333%2Ed2Gu6I)**

name: Dan Goldman
subject: The real emergency in DC
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York**
body:
|
#
---
Peter, Last week, Donald Trump announced that he would deploy the National Guard in our nation’s capital. Trump can claim all he wants that this move is to make DC safer — but we know that’s total bogus. _If DC safety were truly Trump’s priority_ , he would not have granted pardons to 1,500 violent criminals who invaded the Capitol on January 6th and injured more than 140 brave police officers. _If DC safety were truly Trump’s priority_ , he would not have withheld $1.1 billion of federal money for DC’s budget — including law enforcement funding. _If DC safety were truly Trump’s priority_ , he would simply have added more law enforcement officials, not the military. **As is becoming routine, Trump is distracting from his own problems (the Epstein Files) through increasingly authoritarian conduct. ** Trump isn’t deploying the National Guard to our nation’s capital to make our streets safer; violent crime in DC is at a 30-year low. He’s doing it to distract from his tanking poll numbers, a sinking economy, and his likely inclusion in the Epstein files. What’s happening in DC is a calculated political maneuver designed to sow fear, intimidate opposition, and normalize using the military for domestic purposes — one of the first moves used by dictators. While Trump’s allies in Congress — including New York’s House Republicans — stay silent, we must stand firm to demand transparency, justice, and real measures that prioritize the safety of all. **[Will you join me in this critical battle by contributing to our campaign? Your support will help us continue our fight for democracy and ensure that we hold those in power accountable for their actions.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113669206/564550463/1699199121?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250820_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzJkYWEwYS1lMjdkLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRhOWQyM2UiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cWp_dQ5fwb8IFExgaNXrzzh44sPXgFiea9K1zbiYfPM=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113669207/564550464/1699199121?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250820_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzJkYWEwYS1lMjdkLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRhOWQyM2UiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cWp_dQ5fwb8IFExgaNXrzzh44sPXgFiea9K1zbiYfPM=)

clean_body: | Peter, Last week, Donald Trump announced that he would deploy the National Guard in our nation’s capital. Trump can claim all he wants that this move is to make DC safer — but we know that’s total bogus. If DC safety were truly Trump’s priority , he would not have granted pardons to 1,500 violent criminals who invaded the Capitol on January 6th and injured more than 140 brave police officers. If DC safety were truly Trump’s priority , he would not have withheld $1.1 billion of federal money for DC’s budget — including law enforcement funding. If DC safety were truly Trump’s priority , he would simply have added more law enforcement officials, not the military. As is becoming routine, Trump is distracting from his own problems (the Epstein Files) through increasingly authoritarian conduct. Trump isn’t deploying the National Guard to our nation’s capital to make our streets safer; violent crime in DC is at a 30-year low. He’s doing it to distract from his tanking poll numbers, a sinking economy, and his likely inclusion in the Epstein files. What’s happening in DC is a calculated political maneuver designed to sow fear, intimidate opposition, and normalize using the military for domestic purposes — one of the first moves used by dictators. While Trump’s allies in Congress — including New York’s House Republicans — stay silent, we must stand firm to demand transparency, justice, and real measures that prioritize the safety of all. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thank you for your continued support. More soon, Dan Dan Goldman for New York PO BOX 3306 New York, NY 10008 United States | mailto:info@dangoldmanforny.com | Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York

name: VoteVets.org
subject: This wasn’t an easy choice
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
We told you yesterday that we are going up with a $500,000 advertising campaign in Virginia, to support Abigail Spanberger.
#
Peter:
We had to make a really tough decision this week, and we’d like to tell you about it. Do you have a minute?
We told you yesterday that we are going up with a $500,000 advertising campaign in Virginia, to support Abigail Spanberger. We spent a lot of time working on that ad so that it speaks directly to the moderates and center left folks (many of them Veterans) that we’re going to need to turn out for Abigail.
We think it could move the needle. But deciding on the content wasn’t the hard part.
The issue is that our fundraising has dropped dramatically in the months since Trump came into office. Honestly, we weren’t sure we’d be able to cover the cost of this campaign and meet the rest of our obligations this year.
But we came together as a team, and we decided to greenlight that ad. Abigail has to win this race, and we’re going to do whatever we can to help.
Now, we made that decision because this team has always stepped up in the past when we’ve asked, when we’ve really needed it. We’re hoping that by being this honest with you, you’ll consider chipping in to help us close the gaps in our budget — and prepare more ads in more races that will move voters and decide elections.
So, how about it? Can we count on you?
[**Use this link to check out the ad we’re running in Virginia, and make a donation to help us keep it going.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-aug25-virginia-ad.nktB3A&refcode=em250820-1-45&t=1&akid=34545%2E3013755%2E9pUz_J)
| [WATCH THE AD](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-aug25-virginia-ad.nktB3A&refcode=em250820-1-45&t=2&akid=34545%2E3013755%2E9pUz_J)
---
Thank you, as always, for your support.
The team at VoteVets

name: Navy Seal Adam Schwarze
subject: Calling us buffoons and motherf*****s
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
You don’t have to give anything, but if you believe America is still worth defending, I hope you’ll enlist with me today. |

---
Peter,

_**“Asshole. Buffoon. Mofo. Wannabe Hitler. Gangster. Vile villain.”**_

Those are just a few words Rep. Jasmine Crockett used to describe President Trump. But let’s be honest: those insults aren’t just about him. They’re about you. About me. About anyone who leans conservative, wears a uniform, or believes in this country’s founding values.

They think we’re backward. They believe we are in the way. And worst of all, they think we’ll take it.

**As a former Navy SEAL Officer and Marine Corps veteran, I’ve faced far worse than name-calling. I’ve deployed to over 70 countries. I’ve led warriors into battle. I’ve endured life-threatening missions.** And the most important lesson I’ve learned? Quiet professionalism always wins the day.

_[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=5c6018604a634cd79a38448bb7b49d6b&_e=vP-2Zl3RiIbzxxgQZ5iCVUDIItfRchmSJI5UtobZMaYAVMrAXZE8QdRltaxffH2dSXE-SM3O58UIonMQ-i_X6N1MU_9ekHRN2TuQEFu15a2nQcOm4_bK6R68R_n3rwt_3SbHbWAjpp_JAG9ZNpbmctF4zO6pXtRVJbjsY1_GzUTpOtv_KcpogiJLWtgTDL9bfE29cwIsL5s1kde7l7eQKEWNhxODRoy05PpM99ywPRYanIgrR3gYn_2iqKCOTv4ArVUHP22Cf2cUueHus-dGadtEvZIWVUPp3qI29NdOv7plzpGNwILdqF836224SOJ8Wvj4FRjLFqDbMqJ17DBGuRUH_bSJHB4XAKNj1rQGIEJDaUmHwo7cn1IbI_5nW0FZ__qQey6Q75uuJcsPrUSceGuj-sjoVlKoj2DQTpb2aZS0sLCkcoUxUtjxdoc3CjCGb8gXuKLU7SxSvuTSJt5Ip8hv1n8yC-eAYbA5eQh_4MYTwUptxHP-Yz2jOZVK7kgX5ZeZiTHA4xPoHOZCp2Alze64xW88H_Q8OawOwR1pdPEMPD43OTMjV0esA6OVd0btqXU4_5yrPx9o_1rQDzVz32BT0AQWxQTTXGxjFZ55Z21O7K0HKNbHm3nkdYVP-PalDX8xa1ITAw7QhqoiUm2MQNOJS7WxEjyEbv3ai29xQ9I4TEqX4d27Necj_OO5IfgrkSUlHXBNoWmNW4JLwiyyHWDB4LdKprTET9gYTStUujU9fugyu8Kwnz879tFaStUV)_
That’s why I’m running for U.S. Senate in Minnesota — not to yell or mock, but to**fight with discipline, humility, and relentless purpose.**

I didn’t plan on entering politics. But the more I came home from deployment, the more I saw a Minnesota I didn’t recognize. Tim Walz’s lieutenant governor — the person I’m running against — isn’t just radically out of step with our values; she embodies the same hard-left extremism that’s trying to rewrite our entire society.

While Walz called for calm in 2020, she was preparing a lawsuit against the Minneapolis Police. As crime soared and businesses burned, she blamed the people trying to hold the line. These are the people in charge now — and they’re not moderating.

**You can close this email and forget my name. Or you can help send the second Navy SEAL to the U.S. Senate — someone trained for chaos, unafraid of the fight, and grounded in service, not ego. **

**[Will you chip in $1, $5, or even $50 to help me win this battle?](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=5c6018604a634cd79a38448bb7b49d6b&_e=vP-2Zl3RiIbzxxgQZ5iCVUDIItfRchmSJI5UtobZMaYAVMrAXZE8QdRltaxffH2dSXE-SM3O58UIonMQ-i_X6N1MU_9ekHRN2TuQEFu15a2nQcOm4_bK6R68R_n3rwt_3SbHbWAjpp_JAG9ZNpbmctF4zO6pXtRVJbjsY1_GzUTpOtv_KcpogiJLWtgTDL9bfE29cwIsL5s1kde7l7eQKEWNhxODRoy05PpM99ywPRYanIgrR3gYn_2iqKCOTv4ArVUHP22Cf2cUueHus-dGacbtXJDpO4NnaGvWH5ZnROiuxnWwDy57aAlMye_ykGJFqkJjMgL3mCrhFDpB3Rl37syAQ20jPbQ4dnxXJaLCVan0vDuyNt0oLpaSJbcxRl-OcfSXjGdqXvTaY-q9qIUomaFQHNBsofVAzsSmGXasjnhM4wBwS670c_luhMIPGgYfh6CggFPaEXX9Lz8waEj1_uXXmMfzpCZMMEczazyu5CpliSXd4IkR4wscoijukJMTk5HeIXWzKSr9G90giTLbPYiKkHVKsSdYYf8VdH9rXa5SLhhkKebb5AOFZAqPRoKWAcw6iGylxUiPNgrE62OORkaclyEAuHkhcAJjD2Fh06hWHkbDbh1DIeAhPgp0LWJ9yim5IlFwL6wW4Of8wWXO-h3cenPOnaNcrUcFY18AsyPo9LocJd8HpKWLE9Qq7zXFBa8r_7XLT42D_fOy0cW2phQj9XhXNyAswZMvwqQ_Q6Q6LVdSEZPfrloFI4wQXnm2)**

I grew up playing hockey. **I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday in October 2001, less than two months after 9/11.** I served because I love this country, wanted to make my mom proud, and knew I had something to give. Twenty-four years later, I still feel that calling. It’s just a different kind of battlefield.

This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t gone red in a presidential election since 1976. And I know I’m the underdog — just a regular guy from Minnesota who’s been asked to do extraordinary things one more time. With your help, I know we can flip this Senate seat for the Republican Party - but that will require you getting involved TODAY. And you have a choice.

__ I’ve earned advanced degrees from Harvard and Johns Hopkins. I’ve trained in nuclear deterrence. I’ve worked across cultures, built teams under pressure, and led calmly when it mattered most. But none of that compares to the challenge ahead of me now — earning your trust and building a movement powerful enough to flip this seat.

**I’m not asking for a blank check. I’m asking for a chance.**

[**CONTRIBUTE WHAT YOU CAN →**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=5c6018604a634cd79a38448bb7b49d6b&_e=vP-2Zl3RiIbzxxgQZ5iCVUDIItfRchmSJI5UtobZMaYAVMrAXZE8QdRltaxffH2dSXE-SM3O58UIonMQ-i_X6N1MU_9ekHRN2TuQEFu15a2nQcOm4_bK6R68R_n3rwt_3SbHbWAjpp_JAG9ZNpbmctF4zO6pXtRVJbjsY1_GzUTpOtv_KcpogiJLWtgTDL9bfE29cwIsL5s1kde7l7eQKEWNhxODRoy05PpM99ywPRYanIgrR3gYn_2iqKCOTv4ArVUHP22Cf2cUueHus-dGaQsZ_aFvC2_fURu2SoAi7zjaLslZn7vkmbeuZa6rr9UzaLEAcbYU53BN8Ee6pfDOw7moJo83Sk20dOJbYER_iu2xURBBpJSatyKGIqXGr6i4tpaakm8K3MEnZtSGgIF0EyHm3SBKIqqYwFk0vPxWxO6CFwaVXZwo8SM-7o76-sh-2I4JOwAuNocJcEgmeNKRFfNEd9dCDmRjywmhKCWS3MMQ-Q5DZX4iUq2L2hjhXp0q8JUd_PCnOftZPeosLlmxXlrU4CWfG_8Uh7m_UWQLgrB9DFc92r9DzK-WyOnPrLWdBmbQ8d7Jt0nstaufNZe5hiX1qvccnxzrp6p8gMZgSOf12zb7c58S-6POc4PUvj_4v_Fx5fGJ-RsQYswkRBgoId_SaifVHu3bIh6DksaLgEj5ELIA_mEYjQ7SqQtM48r67Jex33EHVJvI0VIRzSDMlMmEu1_7ne16sJj4zAnkGE85JyjX_owz4x115_ImDrZk)

name: Wes Moore
subject: From the Oval Office
body:
[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=482%2E1086043%2EbH2WsO ]Wes Moore for Governor[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1002&akid=482%2E1086043%2EbH2WsO ]Wes Moore for Governor

Donald Trump attacked me and the city of Baltimore last week from the Oval
Office, and it’s not the first time he’s done it.

In response, we’re counting on a huge spike in grassroots support so my
team can spread the truth about our work.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250820-1?refcode=em-20250820-1&refcode2=482_1086043_bH2WsO&akid=482%2E1086043%2EbH2WsO ]STAND UP FOR MD: DONATE NOW »

The president seems to have a sick obsession with the progress that we’re
making in the state. And
while he’s pulling performative stunts like sending the National Guard
into D.C., in Maryland we’re focused on performance.

Since taking office, we’ve led the country in drops in violent crime,
homicides, and shootings. Our number one priority is making sure people
feel safe in their communities and our policies – from investing in law
enforcement to investing in community violence prevention – reflect that
priority. That’s performance.

Meanwhile, Trump is sending our National Guard – servicemembers that are
trained to be sent into states at times of crisis and emergency – into
cities to perform municipal policing functions. You don’t need to have
served our country like me to know that’s performative.

While Trump attacks us from the Oval Office, in Maryland we’re laser
focused on continuing our work to make our communities safer.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250820-1?refcode=em-20250820-1&refcode2=482_1086043_bH2WsO&akid=482%2E1086043%2EbH2WsO ]So, will you pitch in any amount today to help lead Maryland forward?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250820-1?amount=5&refcode=em-20250820-1&refcode2=482_1086043_bH2WsO&akid=482%2E1086043%2EbH2WsO ]Donate $5 ››

Elevate,

Wes

clean_body: [ ]Wes Moore for Governor[ ]Wes Moore for Governor Donald Trump attacked me and the city of Baltimore last week from the Oval Office, and it’s not the first time he’s done it. In response, we’re counting on a huge spike in grassroots support so my team can spread the truth about our work. [ ]STAND UP FOR MD: DONATE NOW » The president seems to have a sick obsession with the progress that we’re making in the state. And while he’s pulling performative stunts like sending the National Guard into D.C., in Maryland we’re focused on performance. Since taking office, we’ve led the country in drops in violent crime, homicides, and shootings. Our number one priority is making sure people feel safe in their communities and our policies – from investing in law enforcement to investing in community violence prevention – reflect that priority. That’s performance. Meanwhile, Trump is sending our National Guard – servicemembers that are trained to be sent into states at times of crisis and emergency – into cities to perform municipal policing functions. You don’t need to have served our country like me to know that’s performative. While Trump attacks us from the Oval Office, in Maryland we’re laser focused on continuing our work to make our communities safer. [ ]So, will you pitch in any amount today to help lead Maryland forward? [ ]Donate $5 ›› Elevate, Wes You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:

name: Becca Balint
subject: Your voice matters, Peter — no matter your zip code
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont**
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113672788/564553815/-785282667?refcode=AU_RAB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250820_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlM2JkMjcxZi1lNjdkLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRhOWQyM2UiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xxQ-sakx4OpKwWtHVahMo8-I_Iw_gcCQM9qx6nLnQX4=)
---
Peter, No matter what your zip code is, I believe your voice matters. That’s why when I heard that Rep. Elise Stefanik of New York was dodging her constituents, I knew I had to step up. **So, I’m hitting the road with Ayanna Pressley and Paul Tonko this weekend to hold a town hall and hear from New Yorkers directly.** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113672790/564553816/-289842851?refcode=AU_RAB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250820_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlM2JkMjcxZi1lNjdkLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRhOWQyM2UiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xxQ-sakx4OpKwWtHVahMo8-I_Iw_gcCQM9qx6nLnQX4=) The far-reaching impacts of Trump’s disastrous presidency are very real. Your concerns, your stories, your struggles — they all deserve a platform. We don’t have to agree on everything, but at a time of historic and sweeping attacks on our rights and democracy, we must show up and listen to each other. Division won’t win this, but unity and community can. name: Lt. Col. David Flippo
subject: Former Mayor Pro-Tem of Mesquite Endorses David Flippo
body:
Conservative leaders across our district are voicing their support for us! | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/1bbcb97316f1/welcome-to-team-flippo-10339698?e=273c9a71fb)
---
| | [ ](https://electdavidflippo.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aba26b775663b53df718fc1a&id=b26c37f38c&e=273c9a71fb)
---
| | Friend, I am proud to announce that **Wes Borger, Former Mayor Pro-Tem of Mesquite** , has officially endorsed my campaign for Congress in **NV-04.** During his time as a City Councilmember and a Mayor Pro-Tem, Wes Borger was a great conservative and fought for many conservative principles in Mesquite, NV.

Here is what Former Mayor Pro-Tem Boger had to say about our campaign:
---
| [ ](https://electdavidflippo.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aba26b775663b53df718fc1a&id=92a47b7115&e=273c9a71fb)
---
| “I am proud to endorse Lt. Col. David Flippo for Congress. As a combat veteran and proven conservative, David has the leadership skills and dedication to represent Nevada with honor. He will put Nevada first, fight for our communities, and stand with President Trump to protect our freedoms. David Flippo is the right choice for Nevada’s 4th District.”

Our message resonates with the people of Southern Nevada because they understand that Steve Horsford is not fighting for their values in Washington. **I have dedicated my life to service, and now I want to ensure a brighter future for the people of Nevada.** In Washington, I will serve you and deliver **America First policies for our state!**

We have a real opportunity to bring real, positive, conservative change to Nevada, but it will take all of us working together. **Will you join our movement to flip NV-03?**
---
| [CHIP IN TODAY](https://electdavidflippo.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aba26b775663b53df718fc1a&id=4445d41c76&e=273c9a71fb "CHIP IN TODAY")
---
|

subject: More resources, more events.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POLITIVIST ACTION PAC
body:
https://www.movement4freedom.com/home [https://www.movement4freedom.com/home]
Hey Peter,
Today I went for a walk. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f-srs-video?refcode=x_250819_DCOccupation_act]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f-srs-video?refcode=x_250819_DCOccupation_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f-srs-video?refcode=x_250819_DCOccupation_act]
That sentence should never be revolutionary. But as a Black woman residing in occupied DC, it’s not just an act of resistance; it is utterly dangerous.
Leaving one’s house with a buddy should never be a mandate. Not being able to walk your neighborhood without fear of the US military detaining you on domestic soil for no reason other than a fascist agenda should never be the norm. But this is the new reality for 700,000 Washingtonians.
Unfortunately, while our awareness is heightened, it is not unusual nor abnormal. Rather, it is the state of existence that millions of black, brown, indigenous, and migrant communities historically and continuously live in these United States.
Trump’s occupation of DC with the National Guard is absolutely unprecedented, uncalled for, and egregious. This occupation must end immediately! And if you live outside of DC, please heed the call to action and contact your Congressional Representatives now; demand that they support HJ Res 115 [https://docs.google.com/document/d/1XPl-5uxtHj6A06qREmsQbjimMYMzOBdaVeqmXgNwN6o/edit?usp=drivesdk] to end the emergency period.
The National Guard in DC does not make us safer.
Demilitarizing law enforcement, providing affordable housing and healthcare for all, increasing living wages, ensuring equitable and just public education, and making DC the 51st state make us safer.
Movement for Freedom is organizing on the frontlines across America to ensure that all people have access to proper safety and freedom. Please support our work today so that taking a simple walk today, tomorrow, or in the future doesn’t ever put anyone anywhere at risk. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f-srs-video?refcode=x_250819_DCOccupation_act]
Stephany Rose Spaulding
Executive Director Politivist Action PAC
Lead Spokesperson for Movement for Freedom
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f-srs-video?refcode=x_250819_DCOccupation_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_m4f-srs-video?refcode=x_250819_DCOccupation_act] Movement for Freedom is a campaign, founded by former Congresswoman Cori Bush, that calls for radically transforming how we build justice, safety, and liberation through love and solidarity. Every single dollar helps us build, train, and fight for the justice we all deserve.
Want to learn more? Visit our website at Movement4Freedom.com [https://www.movement4freedom.com] .

name: Team Guzman
subject: Virginia Mercury: “the margins here have beenrazor-thin”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman
body:
| | | The _Virginia Mercury_ writes that the razor-thin 51-49 Democratic majority in the Virginia House of Delegates is at stake this November - and our race in VA-HD22 is a key battleground.
They say: Del. Ian Lovejoy, R-Prince William, is looking to hold onto his seat in one of the most closely watched matchups in Northern Virginia. He faces a challenge from Elizabeth Guzman, who represented a neighboring district from 2018 to 2024 and is aiming for a return to the House.
The margins here have been razor-thin. In 2024, Harris edged Trump 49% to 47.9%, and Kaine carried the district by just over 2 points.
Over 6,000 donations from people like you have helped public school mom and social worker Elizabeth Guzman outraise MAGA Republican Ian Lovejoy - putting us in a _strong position_ if we can sustain our fundraising in the final days.
**Defending and expanding our VA House of Delegates this November will strike another blow against MAGA Republicans ahead of the midterms - and national Republicans are pouring MILLIONS into this race to stop us.**
******[Help Elizabeth Guzman WIN one of the top red to blue pickup opportunities nationwide, in a TOSS-UP district with razor-thin margins. clean_body: | | | The Virginia Mercury writes that the razor-thin 51-49 Democratic majority in the Virginia House of Delegates is at stake this November - and our race in VA-HD22 is a key battleground. They say: Del. Ian Lovejoy, R-Prince William, is looking to hold onto his seat in one of the most closely watched matchups in Northern Virginia. He faces a challenge from Elizabeth Guzman, who represented a neighboring district from 2018 to 2024 and is aiming for a return to the House. The margins here have been razor-thin. In 2024, Harris edged Trump 49% to 47.9%, and Kaine carried the district by just over 2 points. Over 6,000 donations from people like you have helped public school mom and social worker Elizabeth Guzman outraise MAGA Republican Ian Lovejoy - putting us in a strong position if we can sustain our fundraising in the final days. Defending and expanding our VA House of Delegates this November will strike another blow against MAGA Republicans ahead of the midterms - and national Republicans are pouring MILLIONS into this race to stop us. **** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. Thanks for your support, Team Guzman | Democrat is running for Delegate in Virginia's 22nd District. She came to America as a single mother, becoming a union leader and one of the first Latinas in the Virginia General Assembly. She passed stronger gun safety laws, expanded collective bargaining rights and paid sick leave, and protected reproductive rights. To donate by check, send to: Friends of Elizabeth Guzman PO Box 74, Nokesville VA, 20182 \- \- Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman. Contributions or gifts to Guzman for Congress are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. | | Sent via . body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32989?t=1011&refcode=email-20250820_v&akid=9204%2E5885482%2EYCnvsP ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis,

The list of billionaires and politicians lining up to take down Zohran
Mamdani is growing by the day.

The latest? Rep. Hakeem Jeffries, the highest-ranking Democrat in the
House, is REFUSING to endorse Mamdani, siding instead with Trump’s MAGA
machine.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33255?source=email-20250820&t=1060&akid=9204%2E5885482%2EYCnvsP ]Headline: Blue no matter who?: Jeffries slammed for 'gutless' refusal
to endorse Zohran Mamdani

Let's get one thing clear: Zohran Mamdani OVERWHELMINGLY won his
nomination by speaking to a broad coalition of New Yorkers ready for
justice and change. But now, rather than honoring the will of Democratic
voters, the establishment is throwing Mamdani under the bus.

We can’t let Jeffries and the party bosses join with Trump to dictate our
politics – and so Our Revolution organizers are hitting the streets across
New York to give Mamdani the grassroots support he needs to win.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32989?t=1057&amount=22&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&refcode=email-20250820nd&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9204%2E5885482%2EYCnvsP ]Can you urgently rush a donation to help us power the grassroots
mobilization needed to make sure Mamdani wins – even as billionaires and
the establishment line up to try to defeat him?

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation to Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32989?t=1042&express_lane=true&amount=9&refcode=email-20250820nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9204%2E5885482%2EYCnvsP ]CHIP IN $9 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32989?t=1043&express_lane=true&amount=22&refcode=email-20250820nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9204%2E5885482%2EYCnvsP ]CHIP IN $22

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32989?t=1047&express_lane=true&amount=66&refcode=email-20250820nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9204%2E5885482%2EYCnvsP ]CHIP IN $66 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32989?t=1048&amount=9&refcode=email-20250820nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9204%2E5885482%2EYCnvsP ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT

Jeffries isn’t alone. He’s just the latest establishment insider to cozy
up to MAGA, joining a growing list of politicians more interested in their
donor networks than in real change.

Mamdani has a proven record of fighting for tenants and workers. And the
fact is, Jeffries and other Democratic leaders want to silence him instead
because they’re scared of what real change looks like.

We will not back down. If Jeffries and his wealthy friends want to make
this a battle for the heart and soul of the Democratic Party, we say:
bring it on. We have people power on our side.

Grassroots dollars – not corporate PACs and not billionaire checks –
fueled Mamdani’s historic primary win. Now, with the political
establishment closing ranks, we need to double down to defend our movement
and win this race.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32989?t=1054&amount=22&refcode=email-20250820nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9204%2E5885482%2EYCnvsP ]Chip in now to ensure Our Revolution can train canvassers, organize
rallies, and get out the vote for Zohran Mamdani in these critical final
weeks.

The establishment may have money, but we have the movement. If we all step
up together right now, Mamdani can make history – and show the rest of the
country how progressives can win, everywhere.

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

name: Rob Bonta
subject: Let’s talk numbers
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642]
body:
[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/4?refcode=em250820&t=1001&akid=1921%2E1088799%2EfuxrI9 ]Rob Bonta

You know about the more than three dozen lawsuits I’ve
filed against Trump since he took office seven months ago, but you might
not know what all that work has done for California — and
you.

Take a look at the impact of our litigation by the numbers:

➡️ $168+ billion: The amount of federal funding restored from a single
early lawsuit in week one
➡️ $12.1 billion: Protected federal grant funding from successive attacks
since week one
➡️ $33,600: Returned for the state for every $1 we’ve been given for this
work
➡️ 15: Court orders currently in effect blocking Trump’s illegal actions

In the 212 days since Trump took office for the second time, I’ve sued him
39 times. I’m proud of that — but not because I set out to sue this
President more than once a week.

I’m proud of it because, as Attorney General, I am unapologetic in my
determination to hold accountable those who brazenly, flagrantly, and
continuously violate the law and the U.S. Constitution.

No one is above the law — not even Donald Trump. When leaders abuse power,
it’s the people who pay the price.

This work is only the beginning. We’re seven months in, we have 41 months
to go, and the stakes are too high to slow down even for a moment.

California will work around the clock to fight for our people, our values,
and our resources every single time they come under attack from this
administration.

But I can’t keep up these fights unless I win re-election next year.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-250820?refcode=em20250820-1921&refcode2=1921_1088799_fuxrI9&akid=1921%2E1088799%2EfuxrI9 ]Will you make a donation of whatever amount is meaningful to you to my
re-election campaign?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-250820?refcode=em20250820-1921&amount=5&refcode2=1921_1088799_fuxrI9&akid=1921%2E1088799%2EfuxrI9 ]Donate $5 »

Thanks for standing with me.

In solidarity,
Rob

[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/1042?t=1002&akid=1921%2E1088799%2EfuxrI9 ][IMG]

Rob Bonta
Attorney General of California

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.robbonta.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=1003&akid=1921%2E1088799%2EfuxrI9
Update Your Information: https://act.robbonta.com/signup/update-your-information/
Unsubscribe: https://act.robbonta.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1004&akid=1921%2E1088799%2EfuxrI9

Rob Bonta for Attorney General 2026
c/o Deane & Company
1700 Tribute Road, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA 95815
info@robbonta.com
